Sans Strings Studio is a first-of-its-kind tech-augmented transmedia studio operating at the convergence of gaming, film, social, and live events. Using real-time technology, we co-produce and develop properties seamlessly across interactive, linear, and live formats.

Our non-linear, scalable toolsets allow us to meet rising demand with lower financial risk while preserving the artistry of human-crafted content.

Our F.E.L.T. system is designed for cross-platform scale from the start, allowing IP holders to easily produce interactive and linear content with one asset pipeline; delivering new forms of entertainment that meet audiences wherever they are — from traditional screens to interactive platforms, social networks and beyond.

name: Shockwave
description: We’re harnessing AI and decades of gaming expertise to create the ultimate destination for daily play!  Daily games are a massive opportunity in the 50B+ casual gaming market. They are subscription driven business, with universal appeal to gamers and non gamers alike. With hundreds of millions of players hits like Wordle have popularized the daily game. We are building games across more categories more genres and new interactive experiences to expand and win this market!

name: Sonatic
description: a desktop assistant that observes your screen, learns how you work, and then does it for you.

name: SoulLink
description:
Virtual friends are the next social revolution, and Daedalia is making it real.
We fuse AAA game tech with emotional AI to craft next-gen companions that look cinematic, act human, and feel alive.

With a proven founder and a high-speed team, Daedalia will bring presence to a billion lives.

name: Sourcerer
description:
Sourcerer is the world’s first fully autonomous AI procurement agent. We help distributors automate their entire sourcing process, from identifying suppliers and negotiating prices, to managing freight and credit terms, all through a single intelligent platform.

Using proprietary AI technology, Sourcerer analyzes product needs, tariff exposure, supplier networks, and logistics constraints to instantly generate optimized sourcing recommendations. Our customers save time, reduce costs, and unlock access to high-quality manufacturers across Asia, Latin America, and the U.S.

Our vision is to simplify global trade by empowering buyers with AI that delivers transparency, cost savings, and unmatched efficiency across the supply chain. By combining procurement, logistics, and trade finance into one intelligent engine, we’re building the application layer of the autonomous supply chain.

name: Taya
description: Wearables today are gadgets. We believe they should be jewelry. Taya is a design-led wearable company founded by ex-Apple engineers and Stanford Product Design grads, creating jewelry-grade hardware that makes AI feel personal, beautiful, and effortless to wear. Our first product brings AI off the screen and into everyday life as a living memory layer, capturing experiences in real time, turning them into a searchable, contextual timeline, and evolving into intelligence that anticipates your future. This is not just a wearable; it’s a new category of computing: the world’s first AI-native memory layer - private by default, socially acceptable, and built by a team that knows how to ship products at Apple scale.

name: URSA Mining
description: At URSA, we're developing a universal, physically intelligent operating system for mining equipment. With our system, mines can transform their existing fleets into autonomous earth-moving robots, boosting revenue and increasing efficiency. Our team consists of world-leading experts in physical AI, robotics, and tele-operations. Founded by a second-generation miner and former particle physicist, URSA is on a mission to build the world's first fully autonomous mine.

name: Variant
description: Our proprietary AI models and design systems transform memes, jokes, fanfics, or even office drama into shareable interactive games. Variant is to interactive media what Facebook is for text, what Reddit is for GIFS, and what Tiktok is for videos.

name: Vega
description:
Vega: AI-Powered Social Orbits

On Vega, you leave notes about friends to unlock notes about yourself. Our models analyze these notes to generate AI-powered social orbits, hyper-curated groups of people who will change your life, surfaced with perfect context like mutuals, trust, and shared experiences.

We’re building the largest textual data moat on human relationships by gamifying the way people leave notes for each other. For the first time, LLMs can analyze millions of raw, human-written notes at scale and turn them into structured & meaningful insights about our relationships.

Magic is other people. Orbit the people who matter!

name: YKB
description:
Speculation is becoming how people engage with culture. Betting & prediction markets are exploding. But they’re built for gamblers, not fans.

Our unlock = Receipts: interactive prompts generated by our agent when news breaks. Fans react, predict, & compete together.

1M+ Receipts created since NFL beta began in September. 140k+ Receipts created in the last week. Starts in sports, expands to all areas of culture.

We believe tapping into this fan energy around takes & predictions will bring speculation beyond just gamblers, to a billion people.

name: Zero Billion
description: Zero Billion is creating BookTok, the fastest growing AI TV show platform. Shows are made in under 1 hour for <$10/min. Already Top 10 on iOS in our category with $2M+ annualized revenue.

name: Zingroll
description:
Zingroll is where creative 20-year olds become Walt Disney's of tomorrow by streaming their Netflix-quality AI-shows/movies to the rest of the world. This is the future of Netflix and streaming at-large.
We all scroll Netflix but fail to find stuff to watch, and then finally settle on whatever is relatively watchable. Zingroll changes this forever. Come talk to us!

name: Acceler8
description:
CEOs spend $400B on AI transformation, consulting, and systems — and still can't answer their most critical workforce questions without months of manual work. What should org look like as humans and AI agents work side by side? Which roles to automate? Where to redeploy talent? Who should step into critical roles?

Today these decisions run through spreadsheets, consultants, and static HR systems. They take months, cost millions, and are outdated before they land. As AI compresses years of org change into quarters, workforce decisions can no longer happen once a year. Acceler8 makes them continuous with agentic AI.

Founders:
• Chinmay is 2nd-time founder, raised $80M from Peter Thiel fund, Goodwater, DST, YC. ~$250M valuation. Scaled to team of 400. IIT Bombay
• Trish was Chief People Officer for 35 markets. Led workforce restructuring for 30 companies as Director at PwC. Stanford GSB

name: Advocate
description: Advocate is the AI care coordination system that turns staff voice notes into coded, compliant, auditable billing packets — unlocking hundreds per patient per month in reimbursement for work facilities already do. No new hires, no workflow changes, pure margin.

name: Alike
description: Alike is killing slack with autonomous agent to agent communication. We're an SF + UK Oxford/MIT team of best friends with the rare combination of expertise this requires. We are live in teams in 10 companies, have 100 companies on the waitlist from word of mouth, and signed 20 design partners such as Exus Renewables ($1B backing from Partners Group, powering Google's data centres), K-ID ($51 million raised from Lightspeed and a16z), and NewtonX ($47M raised, trusted by Microsoft and TikTok).
founders: Addi Haran Diman;Max Van Kleek;Danial Hussain

name: Amdahl
description:
Amdahl helps B2B enterprises make sense of messy go-to-market data — structuring it, operationalizing it, and giving teams one place to build data-driven strategies and execute on them, all grounded in rich customer context.

AI in GTM fails when the input data is wrong. Amdahl is the intelligent context layer that structures and understands your data across Sales, Support, and Customer Success, and channel performance. So teams can act on the right context and drive real revenue outcomes.

Amdahl is coming out of stealth with $4M in pipeline from companies like Instacart, OpenTable, Airwallex, and Stripe.

name: Antihero Studios
description:
💡 Insights: Gen Z play video game to socialize. Social mechanics increases organic growth and retention. Nobody is pushing social play on mobile.
👥 Team: We spent our careers taking complex multiplayer genres and making them accessible to hundreds of millions of players.

Antihero Studios is a mobile game studio pushing the boundaries of social on mobile, founded by former Supercell and King veterans who collectively worked on eight billion-dollar games including Brawl Stars, Clash Royale, and Candy Crush.

We're building MISFITZ, the first mass-market extraction game on mobile, tapping into the fastest-growing genre in gaming.

Beyond the game, we're building Antihero as a player-facing brand like A24 or Liquid Death, powered by an organic creator-driven growth engine. A compounding distribution advantage that grows with every game we make.
founders: Brice Laville Saint Martin;Andre Parodi;Frank Yu Yan

name: August
description:
August is the platform where banking work runs.
Domain-specific AI for commercial banking, turning underwriting, servicing, risk etc. into software.

Usage compounds a proprietary context layer.

Deployed at a +$170B bank. Pipeline growing.

Founded by ex-8200 operators who built military AI systems.
reach out at bar@tryaugust.com

name: Auto
description:
Auto is your AI camera. Turn your photos into Frames - mini apps that help you everyday.

The founding team is ex-Snapchat, has spent years working on camera & creator products and both have significant previous exits.

Frames make your camera intelligent. They know your calories, help you dress better, care for your plants, split the bill, give you travel advice, remember your goals, and anything else you can think of.

And if Auto can't do it yet, you can build a Frame that can.

name: Belong
description:
Belong is pioneering FanTech by building the financial infrastructure of fandom — the financial layer that turns engagement into identity, access, and economic value.

Fans already drive billions in economic activity around the artists they love — across tickets, travel, merchandise, and everyday spending — yet almost none of that value flows back to artists. Belong changes that.

We connect fan identity to financial rails, enabling fans to be recognized and rewarded for their participation — while creating a new, recurring revenue stream for artists from the spending that already exists.

As the world shifts from the attention economy to the engagement economy, Belong transforms fandom into a measurable, bankable system — where identity drives activity, and activity creates value.

Co-founded by Nick Holmsten, former Global Head of Music at Spotify, and Ash Pournouri, who built Avicii into one of the world’s biggest artists.

name: Bilrost
description:
Every credit decision runs on context, OMs, tax, financials, borrower history, the judgment of your best underwriters, scattered across systems that don't talk to each other. The hard part of automating credit was never the doc processing. It was turning that context into something accurate enough to act on and auditable enough to defend.

Bilrost is AI-native infrastructure for commercial credit. We turn the documents behind every deal into a structured context graph that powers the full lifecycle — intake, underwriting, servicing, monitoring — collapsing weeks of work down to minutes, with an audit trail risk and compliance can stand behind.

We've already processed over 10,000 deals and are piloting with top lenders. Because the intelligence lives in the context graph, not a black-box prompt, every output is transparent, traceable, and compounds across lenders into intelligence no single institution could build alone.

name: Bota
description:
Bota is the first AI agent infra for real world context, starting with voice data.

In just 5 weeks, Bota grew from 0 to over $700k in contracted ARR. Our customers include top AI agent companies in sales, healthcare, law, finance, productivity and many more.

The Bota founders sold their previous startup for 8 figures to a top AI hardware company. We built hardware at Stanford, Tesla and Apple, and built infra at Amazon and AWS. We deeply understand AI hardware and B2B. Our vision is to be the default real-world context engine for AI agents.

name: Cascade
description:
Cascade is the AI prediction layer for all construction projects, enabling the physical world to be built faster and financed smarter.

In the last 3 months, we went from 0 -> $201k cARR. 15 companies signed, 11 in the last month. Our first three companies have more than doubled their spend with us.
Our clients have built airports like JFK & LGA, hotels like the Four Seasons & the Ritz, and data centers and nuclear reactors.

Hannia & Joana worked together for many years at another AI startup before starting Cascade. Hannia helped scale Google Pay from zero to 100M users, built AI for Pakistan's COVID response and received an award from the Prime Minister. Joana built ML systems for Google Search and for the UK's largest trading app (scaleup).

Our Head of Growth was ranked the #2 sales rep at Procore, where she drove $3M in ARR in 2025.

name: Cedar
description:
Cedar is building the AI-native professional network. Recruiting is the wedge.

The best talent doesn't apply. It gets referred. But referrals break as companies scale. Not from culture, but from math. Companies like Stripe threw 100 people and $10M a year at this problem. Cedar scales this with agents.

Every 15-minute call maps an employee's trusted professional relationships, surfaces high-signal candidates, and deepens a graph no one else is building. Every customer makes it smarter.

Traction: Closed $3M in revenue while in stealth.

Team: Greg Gunn and Beier Cai built Hootsuite from 5 to 1,500 people and zero to $100M ARR in under four years. Referrals were their best channel. They collapsed at exactly the moment they needed them most.

Advisors: Laszlo Bock (Google), Tiffany Fenster (Stripe), Brendan Browne (Replit).

name: Clair Health
description:
$800K D2C revenue, $18M B2B contracts signed in <8 weeks.

Clair is the first continuous hormone monitor- a jewelry-inspired wrist wearable that helps women understand their bodies and optimize their health. Clair leverages a custom biosensor stack and an in-house female biology world model to track estrogen, progesterone, LH, and PdG. Its unique hormonal insights can be applied to understanding skin health, fertility, mood changes, (peri)menopause, and female athletic optimization.

name: Coalition Systems
description:
Coalition Systems is building the shared coordination layer for the national security ecosystem. The defense software stack is hyper-fragmented, and in the absence of purpose-built infrastructure, military units, agencies, intelligence services, and critical infrastructure coordinate on Signal, WhatsApp, and Google Sheets. None of them connected by software built for the purpose. Coalition OS fixes that.

Founded by an Oxford AI Engineering PhD researcher, a critical infrastructure engineer, and a former US DoD intelligence officer. Backed by General H.R. McMaster, as well as the former Director of the Defense Intelligence Agency, and private defense executives.

name: Concorda
description:
Traction: $210K in annualized revenue in 3-months.

Concorda is building the AI operating system for litigation. Built by two lawyer-engineers (Harvard CS, UChicago Law, Northwestern Law), we saw firsthand that existing legal AI tools sit on top of workflows instead of executing them. We’re building the system that runs litigation end to end, charging for both our legal services (AI-native litigation services) and access to our platform (B2B SaaS).

name: Crebit
description:
Crebit is unlocking the next wave of stablecoin-powered foreign exchange adoption. Stablecoin foreign exchange handles $9T in annual volume yet represents just 1% of global foreign exchange. The bottleneck: blockchain quotes expire in 10 minutes, making it impossible for businesses to lock in rates and guarantee payments.

Crebit built the missing layer: the ability to lock blockchain exchange rates for up to 180 days. Combined with a Silicon Valley Bank partnership, Crebit gives enterprises the rate certainty they need while delivering the cost benefits of stablecoin foreign exchange.

Crebit is processing $2M this month, up 100% MoM, with enterprises signed representing $40M in annual foreign exchange volume for 2026.

Crebit was founded by Stanford and MIT dropouts.

At Stanford, Simmi Sen created award-winning mobile apps. At MIT, Jensen Coonradt was named one of the top 50 hackers in the world and worked as a software engineer for Amazon and NASA.

name: Emanate
description: We're building the AI Revenue Engine for industrial materials companies: autonomous AI agents that run and grow revenue operations end-to-end. Our mission is to help the most important industrial materials companies in America experience exponential growth.

name: Grove Tax
description:
Tax preparers spend 65% of their time not doing taxes. They chase documents, do manual data entry, and manage clients back-and-forth. The result: 75% have retired in the last decade, a third of those left want to quit, and the gap between demand and supply widens every year.

You cannot close that gap with people. You close it with AI agents. Grove builds autonomous agents that handle the full tax return lifecycle: intake, document extraction, return preparation, and delivery.

Grove launched with design customers this past season. Preparers are handling returns through Grove 3x faster and paying 3x more than what they pay for their tax software.

The Grove team is currently in talks with institutional tax companies for partnership opportunities.

name: Hammock
description: Hammock is the only agent that actually helps people save on healthcare and the wellness products they're already buying. Our agent scans their purchases to find eligible expenses they've missed. Through our integrated doctor network, we generate Letters of Medical Necessity (“LMN”) that make wellness purchases like gym, massage, and recovery tech HSA/FSA eligible automatically. No paperwork, no guesswork, just swipe. Save money and invest in your health.

name: Heavi
description:
Heavi is building an AI workforce for the $250B heavy duty repair and service market.

As experienced technicians retire, they take decades of institutional knowledge with them. The result is longer service times, harder-to-find parts, and customers losing thousands of dollars a day when their vehicle isn't moving. Heavi fixes this.

We're live with 5 design partners, saving up to 30% of employee time and improving margins by ~40%, earning us $100,000 contracted revenue.

name: Hotbox
description:
Hotbox is Clay for social commerce. Your next customers are already in your likes, comments, and DMs. Hotbox identifies them, matches them to your ICP through deep-level enrichment, and agentically converts your pipeline to expand your untapped revenue potential.

Hotbox, pre-launch, has $350k ARR and $4M in pipeline in just 2 weeks of sales. Founded by Harpriya (CEO). She's had two exits and sold her last company to Salesforce, built the LinkedIn ranking algorithm, and is a published NeurIPS author.

name: Idilio
description:
Idilio is building the AI Powered Micodrama platform for the 1B Spanish and Portuguese speakers.

Micro-dramas are a 14B and emerging global category, but no one owns the Latam market yet – a culturally rich audience raised on telenovelas and now consuming content on mobile. Idilio creates short-form, serialized stories designed for how people watch today: fast, immersive, and built for bingeing.

But we’re not just a content platform. We’re building the infrastructure behind the next generation of storytelling – combining proprietary data, AI-driven production tools, and a growing content ecosystem to understand what people want to watch and deliver it at scale.

Our goal is to become the platform where serialized stories are created, distributed, and monetized globally.

name: Kaaro
description:
Kaaro is building AI agents for the manual work that still runs railroads, from paperwork and data entry to incident reporting, billing, and other operational workflows that still move through phone calls, email, and tribal knowledge.

The founders are second-generation railroaders who have known each other for over a decade. Before Kaaro, they dropped out of Waterloo and UBC at 18 to join Toma (YC W24, a16z-backed) as its first two engineers, where Sai led product and growth and Gautham led infrastructure and reliability as the company scaled from roughly 20 customers to hundreds.

A few months in, Kaaro is already working with Canada’s largest railroad, CPKC, in late conversations with CN, and building with Gulf & Ohio Railways, a collection of four smaller railroads.

name: Loops AI
description:
Loops builds the commerce intelligence layer for brands deploying agents in AI search, virtual try-on, omnichannel sales, and product enrichment. Our agents turn shopper intent into sales, reduce returns, and drive deeper engagement, at every touchpoint, across every channel.

With richer, AI-readable product data at its core, the Loops intelligence layer makes your catalog and commerce operations instantly accessible to any AI agent or system. Top brands trust Loops to make their commerce smarter, faster, and more revenue-driven from day one.

name: Mercury
description:
We’re ex-Palantir FDEs who’ve deployed agent systems in the real world.

Everyone thinks of multi agent teams. Unless you are very technical, you can’t build one.

Mercury lets anyone deploy a team of agents the same way they manage a team of humans. Intuitively, though our no code interface- with full visibility and control over every action those agents take.

name: Meridian
description:
Every consulting firm runs on knowledge that's unstructured, unsearchable, and lost when people leave. Generic AI tools don't fix this - they're stateless by design, starting from zero every conversation.

Meridian is the system of record that fixes this. Every document organized automatically. Every prior engagement retrievable in seconds. Deliverables generated in the firm's exact templates. After two months, a firm's Meridian instance is irreplaceable.

But the software isn't the bet. The bet is the data - behavioral signal on how consulting work actually gets done. You can't download it. You have to earn it one engagement at a time.

4 months in. ~$700K contracted. 3x growth every month since January. Managing partners referring us unprompted. Won bake-offs against Databricks and Palantir.

name: Miraka
description:
Miraka is an always-on cardiac care team. We deliver clinician-led preventative care, informed by continuous patient data. Our models use health records and wearables to predict emerging risk and our team intervenes before emergencies happen. All services are covered by insurance.

The results: we're reducing readmissions for partner hospitals by >90% and capturing 94% of eligible patients vs the industry average of 10%.

$340k annual run rate in 3 months, 71% MoM appointment growth.

Founded by Nolan Abeyta (Princeton), Kaz Nakamura (Princeton), and Jesse Abeyta (Amazon)

name: Mirror Mirror AI
description: Mirror Mirror AI is the marketplace where fashion models license their likeness and brands get stunning AI-generated imagery featuring real people. Brands come to our platform, digitally cast real fashion models, and upon approval, use their likeness to generate lookbooks and campaigns in hours instead of weeks while models get paid. Our platform already features agency-signed models who have graced the covers of Vogue and Harper's Bazaar. Founded by Yusan Lin, a fashion model turned CS PhD, Mirror Mirror sits at the intersection of an industry desperate to use AI and a workforce demanding to be compensated for it.

name: Modaic
description:
Modaic is building the verification and alignment layer for AI decisions. For judgment-heavy workflows like classification, moderation, and automated evaluation, teams today are stuck choosing between expensive human review and weak automated systems that constantly break on edge cases.

Modaic solves this by scoring how confident a model is in each decision, routing only uncertain cases to humans, and using that feedback to continuously improve it's underlying instructions. The result is decision automation that gets more reliable over time while dramatically reducing the amount of manual oversight required to run it.

name: Modern Industrials
description:
Modern Industrials is building the AI workforce for distribution, starting with the $450B+ building materials industry. Co-founders Austin, Vatsal, and Ankit have known each other for decades. Vatsal and Ankit are brothers and Austin is their childhood best friend. The team combines deep technical and operational experience from McKinsey, Google (MLE), and xAI (MTS).

Their first product (AI Sales Engine) transforms the back office from a manual cost center into a high-margin engine by automating quoting, order entry, and analytics. At scale, Modern Industrials seeks to become the end-to-end OS for distribution across the physical world.

name: Oasiz
description:
Oasiz is an interactive social distribution platform for software. As creation gets easier, software is evolving into a form of content, yet incumbent distribution channels aren't keeping up. Oasiz does for this next generation of AI-native creators what TikTok did for video creators.

Starting with games, the most engaging and naturally social form of software, they launched less than a month ago and users have already spent over 9,000 hours on the platform, surpassed 300K plays, and gone viral on TikTok with over 7 million organic views. They also have partnered with Atari to put their IP on the platform and bring their iconic catalog to this next generation format where games are AI-built and socially distributed.

Highly technical and creative team from Stanford, Berkeley, Google, Tesla, Nintendo, Amazon, and Superhuman. Founders previously hit #1 on the App Store social charts with 500K+ installs.

name: Omi Health
description:
Omi is building the future of pet health—extending pet lifespans through longevity testing and AI-powered, personalized care.

The co-founders met at Harvard Business School and built Omi for their own dogs. Between them, they bring a decade of experience in human healthcare, consumer branding expertise as Chief of Staff at Ritual ($250M+ ARR), and proven product chops as a second-time consumer tech founder.

Gen Z and Millennial pet parents control a $94B market and spend nearly 2x more per household on their pets than prior generations. This generation is also obsessed with their own wellness and longevity—fueling the rise of companies like Function Health and Superpower—and they're bringing that same preventative, data-driven mindset to their pets.

Since launching on March 18, 2000 pet parents joined their waitlist, translating to ~$1M estimated ARR. Join today at www.joinomi.com.

name: Panorama
description:
Panorama is an AI platform that mines workplace data - Slack, email, docs, etc. - to surface hidden team workflows and deliver personalized AI adoption recommendations through a TikTok-like feed reducing friction and driving adoption from the bottom up.

The company's CEO Jingwei Hao, lead and scaled technology adoption at Cash App and Lyft, and Jaclyn Lunger the CTO, is an MIT ML scientist and first employee at Lila Sciences ($1.3B), whose AI work on green hydrogen and carbon capture was patented and featured by the New York Times.

name: PartyHat
description: AI threats are on the rise but consumer cyber is stuck in the 90's. Partyhat is shaking up this stale $10B+ market with wild, unforgettable marketing and a reinvented, modern product built with AI-powered tech.

name: PayPath
description:
$2M ARR in under 12 months. PayPath is building the AI operating system for the $350 trillion global debt market. While debt is one of the largest financial systems on Earth, it remains trapped in a world of spreadsheets, manual workflows, and legacy tech. PayPath replaces these outdated systems with agentic AI infrastructure, transforming debt management from a fragmented cost center into a high-margin, automated engine.

With over a dozen enterprise customers onboarded and more than half a billion dollars in assets managed on the platform, PayPath is bringing unmatched scale and efficiency to the modern debt economy.

name: PicPet
description:
Over 200k DAU and 45% D90 bounded retention
A social virtual pet where you feed it photos.

name: Piper-ai
description:
General contractors make critical pricing and risk decisions from thousands of fragmented bid documents under extreme time pressure.
On projects worth hundreds of millions of dollars, even small mistakes in scope, pricing, or risk can erase millions in margin.

Piper helps them understand what they are actually bidding: what must be accounted for, where the hidden risk is, and what contradictions could become costly mistakes.

In 12 weeks since launch, Piper has reached $100K+ ARR and built a $1M+ pipeline with multi-billion-dollar contractors.

name: Pluvo
description:
When deploying AI in the Office of the CFO, the biggest challenge isn't the frontier model. It's the context.

The information needed to make decisions lives across disconnected systems, spreadsheets, reports, and institutional knowledge. Without a trusted foundation, AI struggles to deliver accurate, secure, and auditable outcomes.

Pluvo provides a secure, governed home for the context and relationships that define how your business actually works. We unify data, business logic, definitions, processes, and institutional knowledge into a shared semantic layer that powers both people and AI.

Because your context lives outside the underlying LLMs, it remains transparent, auditable, portable, and model agnostic. Whether you use OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, or future AI systems, every model operates from the same trusted foundation.

Pluvo helps finance teams deploy AI with confidence by transforming business context into a secure, governed, and durable strategic asset.

name: Prior Foundry
description:
Prior Foundry is an applied AI lab advancing society-level simulations.

We build a decision intelligence platform for governments and policy teams, simulating how real populations respond to policy before millions of people feel it. Our platform provides on-demand behavioral intelligence that used to take consultants months, if not years to produce. Built on our research at the Stanford lab that pioneered human behavior simulation.

In just two months, we got to 60k ARR, a partnership with the OECD across 38+ member governments, a pilot with Allegheny County in the US, and >1M in pipeline through a distributor.

name: Quanto
description:
Quanto is the AI workforce for accounting firms, automating client prospecting, onboarding, and bookkeeping cleanup so firms can scale revenue and margins without adding headcount.

Built with AI from the ground up by CPAs for accountants, with $800K contracted ARR and a $5M+ pipeline.

name: Quinn
description:
Traction
• $200K → $1.1M+ ARR in ~5 months
• 65+ customers
• Growing ACV + multiple 6-figure deals
Why Now
• Frontline workforce (2.7B people) lacks scalable training infrastructure.
• Training is a $400B market, primarily to pay humans to train other humans.
• We're turning this massive services industry into software margins.

Team
• Ben Anderson (Amino: 20M+ users, $70M raised, acquired)
• Arlen Marmel (early Hulu + Crunchyroll, $1B+ exit)

Hit LIKE to get in touch!

name: Quo Labs
description:
The average nursing home costs over $100,000 a year, and most families would do anything to avoid it.

We're building the infrastructure layer for eldercare that makes that possible. Our vision is making every senior's home fully agentic: AI-powered systems that monitor, assist, and protect the elderly around the clock, so they can age in place safely and independently while saving their families millions of dollars in care costs.

We're starting with Sam, an AI assistant that handles what families would otherwise pay caregivers thousands of dollars per month to do: daily check-ins, complex medication and appointment reminders, all while keeping seniors connected with their families through hands-free texts and voice calls.

name: SafeWorld
description:
SafeWorld is an evaluation platform for robots operating around people. We generate rare but critical scenarios to uncover failure modes, quantify risk, and help robotics teams deploy with confidence.

Our team combines a Carnegie Mellon professor and NSF CAREER Award winner in robot safety with repeat founders who have built and scaled AI companies.

If you believe safety is important to the deployment of Physical AI we'd love to chat

https://safeai-lab.github.io/

name: Sellara
description:
Sellara is building custom AI agents for financial institutions. Our AI outperforms leading LLMs by 60%+ and runs 30x faster.

We automate repetitive, manual workflows across front, middle, and back offices. We're working with banks, broker-dealers, hedge funds, asset managers, and wealth managers to make complex financial workflows feel automatic.

Sellara is becoming the AI lab for financial institutions to turn training data into trading data.

name: Sentra
description:
Enterprise General Intelligence starts with memory. Sentra captures the interactions where work actually happens, meetings, messages, emails & agent traces,  and turns them into a shared organizational memory. That memory becomes the foundation for intelligent models that can understand and coordinate work across the entire company.

As multiple time founders with backgrounds from MIT, IBM, and Caltech, we assembled a team of engineers and researchers from Google, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft, Apple, and Databricks to tackle this problem with us.

Simula helps AI apps monetize through native, interactive ad experiences.

Launched three months ago, we’re integrating into AI apps covering 5M+ DAUs and 100M+ downloads, representing ~$10M+ in net revenue at full rollout.

Traditional ads break AI products. We replace them with formats that feel like part of the experience – AI brand ambassadors, games users play with AI companions, and more.

The team has built and scaled dozens of apps and worked across AdTech at Google and Bain Capital.

Currently over $3 million in ARR, with 200% NRR and 75% MoM growth.

We are solving one of the biggest problems for subscription companies: churn. Most brands see a huge drop in subscribers in the first few months. We developed a novel approach to solve this by building an AI model for retention.

We are currently live and managing retention for unicorn-status consumer companies, processing millions of interactions daily.

name: Smart Bricks
description:
Smart Bricks is an applied AI lab building agentic reasoning systems for real estate investing. We develop and deploy autonomous agents, multi-model reasoning, and continuous learning loops to transform how real-world assets are discovered, evaluated, and managed.

We’re already at a ~$12M revenue run-rate, backed by a16z Speedrun alongside leading angels from OpenAI, Google DeepMind, and Anthropic.

Our team brings experience from Blackstone, Goldman Sachs, McKinsey, and OpenAI — combining institutional investing expertise with frontier AI research.

Smart Bricks functions as intelligence infrastructure: turning fragmented, opaque property markets into computable, decision-ready systems.

name: snag
description:
snag is the AI-native sublet marketplace for GenZ. We generated $6.5m in requests in the last 30 days, growing 40% m/o/m, with 3k listings in NYC!

Today millions exchange sublets on FB groups, IG, Craigslist. We're turning this raw, messy sublet culture into an organized, high-trust marketplace. Our proprietary AI model converts social posts into high quality listings instantly, and matches renters with the right sublet within seconds. snag fills the gap between Airbnb and Zillow, making housing liquid for a new generation of renters.

Cofounded by Selin Sonmez and Niko Georgantas, repeat consumer founders in the space, backed by world class investors like General Catalyst and others.

name: Snapp Stats
description: Snapp is a personalized sports feed powered by AI. It brings you the right content, news, highlights, and insights in real time, and lets you instantly go deeper with analytics when you want. No more tab-hopping. No more guesswork.

name: Sparta
description:
Sparta is an intelligent data delivery layer that dynamically optimizes large-scale data transfers across clouds, clusters, and distributed systems.

Modern AI workloads, streaming platforms, and high-throughput applications lose time and money to inefficient data movement. Static transfer settings fail under fluctuating network conditions, leading to idle GPUs, longer processing times, wasted bandwidth, and rising infrastructure costs.

Sparta replaces fixed heuristics with a real-time reinforcement learning engine that continuously adapts based on live network signals. The result is faster transfers, better bandwidth utilization, lower energy consumption, and more predictable performance without requiring infrastructure rewrites.

Designed as a drop-in layer that works on top of all existing storage and transfer tools, Sparta helps AI labs, media platforms, gaming companies, and cloud-native enterprises ensure that data delivery never becomes the bottleneck to performance or scale.

name: Straia

$4.5M committed ARR* in 4 weeks.

Straia is the agentic AI platform replacing legacy software across higher education. We unify siloed institutional data and deploy AI agents to analyze and act on that data.

Building the AI-native operating system for every college in America.

*In contracting

Create podcasts, audiobooks, or structured audio experiences on any topic. Customize everything, from length and voice to language. Ask questions at any moment and get answers in real time.

Not sure what to create? SUN knows what to play. Connect the apps you already use, and it continuously adapts to your interests, delivering audio tailored to you.

Built by Artin Bogdanov (Harvard CS), Mehmet Günhan Ertosun (Stanford PhD in AI), and Amy Hua (founding engineer at Amazon Podcasts), SUN combines deep technical expertise with real insight into how people consume audio.

Launch Tweet: https://x.com/aaronistan/status/2041909335220220252?s=20

We’re building robots that automate everyday chores at home. Our first product, Lume, looks like a normal lamp. When needed, it transforms into a pair of robotic arms that can complete tasks like folding laundry. When it’s done, it returns to being a lamp, so your home feels unchanged, except the chores are taken care of.

Our team comes from top robotics programs like Stanford, the University of Toronto, and Berkeley, with experience building hardware at Tesla, Nuro, and AMD, and design experiences at Apple and Harvard.

The product is ready. Our lamps can fold laundry autonomously, and we are ready to begin manufacturing and shipping this summer.

We have strong early demand, with hundreds of pre-orders and millions of dollars in waitlist. The product has generated millions of organic views online and has attracted attention from Jeff Bezos, Tim Cook, Will.I.Am, Dario Amodei and others.

No engineer interviews, no annual reconstruction scramble — contemporaneous, source-linked evidence built to stand up to IRS/CRA/HMRC scrutiny, surfacing qualifying work that gets missed.
Live in the US/UK/FR/CA, with clients like Paddle and incident.io and backed by finance professionals like ex-Twilio CFO and Kraken's Head of Tax.

Our end-to-end post-training infrastructure captures expert decisions directly and applies them on top of base models, allowing teams to steer production models continuously without error-prone workflows, heavy datasets, and massive labor.

$500K ARR, doubled in the last 30 days
$2.5M in Pipeline

45 days to +800k acres, representing +$155m in GMV and $8m in potential revenue

Vereda addresses one of the largest structural inefficiencies in Brazilian agriculture (US$700b Market): distribution. It is building the most efficient way for suppliers to access fragmented farm demand in Brazil.

Through a WhatsApp native procurement agent, Vereda captures and structures farmers’ purchasing intent, enabling direct distribution from manufacturers with structurally lower cost to serve for suppliers and improved pricing for farmers by eliminating inefficiencies in the distribution layer.

By owning the producer interaction layer, Vereda generates proprietary datasets on demand, behavior, and credit, enabling dynamic offer optimization, embedded financial services, and expansion across all categories of farm spend over time.

In regulated industries, one bad message means millions in fines, lost clients, lawsuits. ZeroDrift makes sure it never leaves your organization.

Team: Built the first AI platform for finance (2014), co-created Microsoft Bing AI, Goldman Sachs Digital Platform, Google Chrome Enterprise. 6 FTEs, NYC.

Traction: Launched Feb 2026. $150K ARR, 50%+ MoM growth, $2M qualified pipeline (Franklin Templeton, EQT Partners, Nasdaq, Citi, Two Sigma, Vanguard). SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR compliant. Product built, ready to scale.

name: Belong
description:
There is more than one American Dream. Owning your home is one of those dreams. It's been an aspiration for a long time, and we know that millions struggle to achieve it. But there are other American Dreams.

Some of these dreams are about living differently than previous generations - for whom owning was everything. We may want to use our cash to start a business. We may want to experience new places. We may not have the money yet for home ownership, but still dream of living in a glorious home where we can put down roots and experience the sense of belonging.

This is a new mindset.

We are a community of people looking for a dream rental, and people who own properties and are looking for people who will love them. The old-school terms "landlord" and "tenant" don't apply in our world. Here, there are people who want to adopt homes for a while, and people who have wonderful residences ready for them.

Our owners are not giant holding companies that own thousands of homes and view them as pure financial assets. There's a level of mutual respect and caring in our world that simply doesn't exist in the transactional, brutalist world of landlords and tenants.

We manage properties for our owners, and treat the people who live there with the attention, speed and concern they deserve. And we support those who are living an ownership-free lifestyle by creating a true community everywhere we are. Our residents are connected not only to each other, but also to the local merchants, events, and institutions that give communities their unique character. Before, renters often felt like second-class citizens; with us, they are an immediate and integral part of the community.

So, whether you own property and you're looking for someone to live in it and love it, or you're looking for a home where you can put down roots for however long you're planning to be there, we are the answer.

Renting is the new American lifestyle. We didn't create it, but we're making it sing.

name: Keybase.io
description:
Keybase is working hard to bring all the promise of public key cryptography to regular people. We're solving three challenging problems in parallel, building out: (1) an open and auditable PKI based on social network identities; (2) support for all mobile and desktop platforms, so that every device can have its own private key; and (3) viral sharing applications that use clean UX to hide the complexity we've all come to associate with public keys.

We want to change the way security works at a deep level, so that sharing, end-to-end security, and cloud hosting can peacefully coexist. And we won't be happy with isolated enterprise installs, or appeal to just hardcore tin-foil hats. We're aiming for widespread adoption: first among programmers, and eventually among consumers at large.

name: Mediachain
description:
The goal of Mine is to make metadata for digital images persistent and discoverable anywhere images exist. Today, information about an image, such as authorship or context, is quickly lost as it is shared across the internet. An image may start its life illustrating a news article on the New York Times, annotated with the photographers name and an insightful caption, but it quickly gets stripped down of any valuable context as it propagates different channels of the internet. We effectively know less about an image as it goes viral and reaches more people

Mine wants to leverage the phenomenon of social image sharing in the production of a visual knowledge project. Mine uses computer vision to connect the thousands of different appearances of an image, over a thousand contexts and across any platform into one source image: where everything about that image can be collected and known. The entire ripple an image makes across the world is shown under one heading and the Mine community filters what information is most important.

Our goal is to disinter mediate media consumption and interaction, which today relies on centralized platforms such as Facebook and Twitter which monetize their users and all the content that flows through them with little benefit to the communities that create that value. This is why we are building our dataset on top of the blockchain, the same technology that enabled two people to exchange a financial asset with no middleman in the case of Bitcoin. We have specified a proposal for our open dataset in the Canonical Content Registry: https://github.com/mine-code/canonical-content-registry

name: NEAR
description:
NEAR Protocol is a community-operated cloud which forms the backbone infrastructure of the Open Web stack and supports an entirely wave of new applications that will be built using open technologies.  This is the same scope of impact achieved by early Internet protocols like TCP/IP or cloud-based hosting services like AWS.

NEAR will succeed where prior generations of platforms like Ethereum have not because the technology is finally at a place which allows the level of usability necessary to achieve mainstream adoption and the at-scale performance necessary to support it.

Let's be clear: NEAR isn't a side chain, an ERC20 token or a highly specialized task-specific blockchain, it is nothing less than a brand new and fundamentally reimagined layer 1 protocol designed to independently power the base of the Open Web stack.  Unlike other projects in the space which are glorified research projects or hand-wavy marketing pitches, NEAR is being built by a global collective of world champion competitive programmers and successful entrepreneurs who know how to take technology to market.

We're looking for only the very best, world-class engineers and startup veterans to join this journey.  Do you have what it takes to change the world?

Referral Source:  Cold email to Marc A

Founder Sketches:  2 '08 CS grads from Berkeley

Basic idea:  Enabling HPC customers today (have the vision to make this generally available to all cloud application) to access a cloud grid.  They insert a few lines of python code (extending to Java soon) into the application code to fetch the relevant data and run queries on a grid sitting on top of AWS.  Apparently (which was news to me), 25% of AWS's instances are in fact HPC today.  The vision - which may sound familiar - is to ultimately offer Computing Power as a Utility

Does the product exist:  Yes - in beta

Metrics:  1,700 beta users today

Amount Raised:  $35k grant from Lightspeed in Jan 09

Amount raising:  $650k seed round

Enthusiasm:  8 - very smart entrepreneurs with a very simple value prop today, but with a strong vision for how to make this a general purpose public or private cloud platform.

description:
We have created a breakthrough phone verification solution that detects and reports phone fraud. It is based on our research that shows that every phone call
has a fingerprint hidden within the audio. This is based on over 50 artifacts such as noise, codec and line loss. This fingerprint can authenticate legitimate callers
and tag fraudulent callers. It can also determine the phone type (landline, cell or VoIP) and location of the caller.

Our solution operates completely and independently at one end requiring no modifications to existing telecommunication infrastructure. Our real-world testing has also shown that these fingerprints are resistant against attacks.

name: Shield AI
description:
Shield AI's mission is to protect service members and innocent civilians with artificially intelligent systems.

Our initial focus is on the last several miles of the battlefield where we lack capability to collect intelligence. This area is beyond the view of high altitude sensors, too dangerous for people, and too complex for robots to navigate. Consequently, service members are asked to make life altering decisions with limited information. Mistakes are made that cost lives and incur massive collateral damage. Such mistakes cause the United States to lose support of local populations and make conflict unwinnable. This problem is widely recognized throughout the DoD, including the most senior leadership.

To address this problem, we are building swarms of small intelligent robots to collect the critical missing information. The systems will scour indoor and outdoor spaces in search of threats and innocent people. They will be deployed by people, trucks, ships, aircraft, and other robots. These robots will become more numerous in combat areas than soldiers. Synthesized information will be presented to service members who will be empowered to make decisions leading to intended outcomes without collateral damage. Conflict becomes less brutal.

By putting choice in the hands of students and providing a program that helps students identify purpose and direction for themselves, Verto empowers students as they choose and enter college. Verto is where students own their first year of their learning, find purpose, and empower their mindset for a fulfilling college and professional career.
founders: Mitch Gordon;CEO & Co-Founder; Ben Welbourn;COO & Co-Founder; Mallory Meiser;CPO & Co-Founder

name: Voxy
description: Personalized, Cloud-Based Language Learning Platform Changing the Way the World Learns EnglishVoxy is changing the way the world learns English. As a language learning platform offering personalized, adaptive, and career-aligned courses, Voxy provides truly personalized English language instruction to corporations, educational institutions, and governments across the globe. Based on more than 30 years of academic research, Voxy’s solution meets the needs of every user by combining patented technology that adapts lessons in real time with personalized live instruction—all delivered in a fully mobile experience.

name: Amplify
description: Amplify develops next-generation instructional programs that blend research-driven pedagogy with engaging digital experiences. Designed for science, reading, and math, Amplify’s solutions help educators personalize learning, track progress in real time, and spark curiosity in every classroom. The company partners with districts across the U.S. and internationally to power a more dynamic and data-informed approach to teaching.

name: Andela
description: Andela connects companies with top software talent worldwide through a proprietary AI-matching platform that streamlines sourcing, vetting, and hiring. By leveraging machine learning to assess both technical skill and cultural fit, Andela enables faster, smarter hiring of remote engineers—helping companies build high-performing teams at scale.

name: Ascent
description: Ascent offers innovative, student-centric lending products that go beyond traditional credit metrics, empowering students to fund their education based on future potential and not just past financial history. With options including non-cosigned loans and income-based repayment models, Ascent is building a more equitable path to higher education and long-term financial wellness.

name: Bloc
description: Bloc offered intensive, mentor-led coding bootcamps designed to help adults transition into software development careers. Through fully online programs and one-on-one guidance from industry professionals, Bloc combined flexible learning with real-world project work, pioneering a scalable, outcomes-driven model for tech workforce development.

name: BloomBoard
description: BloomBoard partners with school systems to offer educators fully accredited, on-the-job master’s degrees and credentialing programs, paid for by the district. Through a unique, competency-based model, teachers earn career-advancing qualifications by demonstrating skills in their daily work, helping districts retain talent while building a stronger, more future-ready workforce.

name: Brainly
description: Brainly combines community-driven knowledge sharing with advanced AI tools to help students get unstuck, understand faster, and build confidence across subjects. With over 350 million users globally, Brainly delivers instant, step-by-step help through an intelligent blend of human input and machine learning, transforming how students learn outside the classroom.

name: BrightBytes
description: BrightBytes is a comprehensive fully-managed data and analytics platform for K-12 educators that empowers school-wide improvement through predictive and actionable data-driven insights. The platform includes solutions data warehouses, dashboards, analytics, and usable insights.

name: Brilliant
description: Brilliant helps learners master STEM concepts through hands-on, visual lessons that teach by doing, not just watching. With a focus on logic, math, data science, and engineering, Brilliant’s interactive courses empower students and professionals alike to think critically, solve problems, and build foundational skills for a rapidly evolving world.

name: Class Technologies Inc.
description: Class transforms virtual instruction by adding education-specific tools to Zoom, enabling more structured, interactive, and accountable online learning. Designed for K–12, higher ed, and workforce training, Class helps educators deliver engaging instruction at scale in a digital-first world.

name: ClassDojo
description: ClassDojo helps educators create positive classroom cultures and keep families engaged through messaging, student portfolios, and real-time updates. Used by millions of teachers and parents around the world, ClassDojo makes school communities more connected, transparent, and supportive, both in and beyond the classroom.

name: Climb Credit
description: Climb Credit provides student loans and financing solutions for programs that deliver measurable career outcomes, ranging from tech bootcamps to trade schools. By aligning repayment with post-graduation earning potential, Climb helps learners access high-ROI training while enabling schools to scale programs that lead to real economic mobility.

name: CodeHS
description: CodeHS equips middle and high schools with everything they need to teach computer science — standards-aligned curriculum, teacher tools, professional development, and student progress tracking. Designed to be accessible for both new and experienced educators, CodeHS is helping school systems nationwide build the next generation of programmers and problem-solvers.

name: Cohort Guidance
description: Cohort provides streamlined, group-based college counseling designed for high school students navigating the application process. Students work in small, live peer cohorts alongside recent graduates from top universities, receiving structured support through 1-on-1 mentoring, group workshops, proactive nudges, and deadline management, all for a flat annual membership.

name: Coursera
description: Coursera is a leading MOOC platform that partners with top universities and education institutions around the world to offer courses, certifiates, and degrees in a variety of fields. It offers both a consumer product and an enterprise product with curated course selections targeted at employees.

name: Creative Live
description: CreativeLive offers expert-led video courses in photography, design, business, and personal growth, built for creators, entrepreneurs, and lifelong learners. With a focus on practical skills and inspiring instruction, CreativeLive empowers millions to turn passion into profession through accessible, high-quality content taught by leading voices in their fields.

name: Curio
description: Curio empowers K–12 and higher-ed learners with interactive educational experiences — like dissecting virtual frogs or exploring historical sites — in a distraction‑free virtual environment. Built with an intuitive, iPad‑style interface, the Curio platform enables schools to incorporate STEM, history, and career simulations with zero training required.

name: DIY
description: DIY.org offers thousands of hands-on projects, challenges, and video courses that help kids explore interests ranging from animation to engineering. With a safe online community and built-in mentorship, DIY inspires self-directed learning while nurturing creativity, confidence, and curiosity in young makers around the world.

name: Desmos
description: Desmos builds powerful, intuitive digital math tools that help students explore concepts dynamically and deepen understanding. Trusted by millions of teachers and learners, Desmos brings math to life in classrooms and online through real-time feedback, accessibility features, and thoughtfully designed instructional content.

name: Dreambound
description: Dreambound helps individuals discover and enroll in high-impact vocational programs through a personalized, AI-guided experience. Focused on accessibility and outcomes, Dreambound connects learners to healthcare, tech, and skilled trade training while providing coaching and accountability to help them stay on track and succeed.

name: EdSurge
description: EdSurge provides educators, entrepreneurs, and policymakers with in-depth journalism, trend analysis, and research on the intersection of education and technology. As a trusted voice in the field, EdSurge helps decision-makers navigate change and make more informed choices about the tools shaping classrooms and campuses.

name: Edmodo
description: Edmodo was a free service that allowed K-12 teachers to create and maintain safe and secure classroom communities. Edmodo was used to share educational content, manage projects and assignments, handle notifications, conduct quizzes and events, and facilitate highly engaging learning experiences.

name: EduNav
description: EduNav is the most sophisticated academic planning solution in the higher education market, providing personalized, real-time educational planning. Its SaaS platform offers a range of modular services using proprietary algorithms to optimize educational plans and improve student persistence and on-time graduation rates.

name: Emergence
description: Emergence is an agentic AI platform that lets business users describe goals in plain English, and in response, the system automatically generates, orchestrates, and runs fleets of specialized AI agents. Designed for tasks like data pipeline creation, QA automation, and cross-system integration, Emergence’s CRAFT tool delivers fast, scalable workflows with built‑in planning, memory, compliance, and continuous self‑improvement.

name: Empath
description: Empath uses AI and machine learning to map and update employee skills in real time. By integrating with HR systems, it helps organizations uncover hidden talent, guide internal mobility, and match people to projects based on actual capabilities. The platform improves retention, boosts productivity, and supports long term career growth.

name: Espark Learning
description: eSpark uses adaptive technology to deliver engaging, standards-aligned lessons for K–5 students in math and reading. With interactive, game-like activities and built-in teacher tools, eSpark personalizes learning pathways for each student, boosting growth while saving educators time.

name: Extern
description: Extern helps students with career discovery and launch through offering short-form professional experiences -- remote externships -- available anywhere and at any time.

name: Feeling Great
description: Feeling Great is a new mobile app inspired by the groundbreaking work of world-renowned Dr. David Burns. Using the power of advanced AI-powered chatbots and interactive courses, the app guides users through a transformative journey toward enhanced self-esteem and emotional well-being.

name: General Assembly
description: General Assembly offers immersive education programs that prepare adults for in-demand roles in fields like software engineering, data science, UX design, and digital marketing. With full-time and part-time options, employer partnerships, and strong job placement support, General Assembly helps learners transition into high-growth careers.

name: Groovetime
description: Groovetime helps users learn, practice, and compete in viral dance challenges through its AI-powered Groovetracker that provides instant feedback. With thousands of daily-updated routines and multiplayer modes, the app turns dance into a social, interactive experience, empowering users to master moves solo or with friends across the globe.

name: Guidepost Global Education
description: Guidepost Global Education operates a cloud-enabled, globally distributed Montessori school network serving students from infancy through secondary years, supported by a centralized services platform that integrates standardized curriculum design, educator training, and operational infrastructure to ensure consistency and scalability across campuses.

name: HireArt
description: HireArt helps companies source, vet, and employ top contract workers with a single, streamlined solution. The platform combines applicant tracking, skills assessment, and compliant employment infrastructure to reduce overhead and improve quality. Employers get the benefits of a trusted staffing partner with full visibility and control.

name: Kalibrr
description: Kalibrr connects job seekers in Southeast Asia with roles that match their skills, experience, and aspirations. Using AI-powered recommendations and an end-to-end hiring platform, Kalibrr helps employers find qualified candidates faster while empowering millions of professionals to grow their careers.

name: Kibeam
description: Kibeam is closing the childhood reading gap through a non-screen platform enabling young learners to engage with existing paper books in highly interactive and novel ways.

name: Landed
description: Landed provides down payment support and financial coaching to educators, healthcare workers, and public sector employees. By partnering with employers and school districts, Landed helps essential workers access homeownership, build long-term wealth, and stay rooted in high-cost communities.

name: Learn Zillion
description: LearnZillion’s platform, curriculum, and software are designed to transform the classroom experience, offering teachers the power of engaging, customizable, classroom-ready tools to foster greater outcomes for students.

name: Learning Machine
description: Learning Machine enabled institutions to issue verifiable academic records and certifications that recipients could own and share. By leveraging blockchain for security and transparency, the platform helped universities, governments, and employers streamline credentialing and reduce fraud.

name: LetMeSpeak
description: LetMeSpeak combines gamified English lessons with blockchain-based rewards, letting users earn tokens as they progress. With AI-driven coaching and immersive storylines, the platform turns language acquisition into an engaging and financially motivating experience.

name: Make School
description: Make School offered a redesigned college experience for aspiring software engineers, combining project-based learning, industry mentorship, and a focus on real-world skills. With income share agreements and strong job placement support, Make School aimed to align student outcomes with institutional accountability.

name: Manga High
description: Manga High is a pioneer in the development of adaptive and embedded-assessment online learning games for math and physics in the middle grades. Free for schools and students, Manga High features high quality games that emphasize rapid-fire learning of different math techniques.

name: MasteryConnect
description: MasteryConnect provides K–12 educators with real-time insights into student understanding through standards-based assessments. The platform supports data-driven instruction by making it easy to identify learning gaps, personalize teaching, and improve outcomes across classrooms, schools, and districts.

name: Mentor Collective
description: Mentor Collective partners with colleges and universities to match students with trained peer, alumni, or professional mentors. Using a research-backed approach and data insights, the platform supports belonging, persistence, and career readiness at scale across the student journey.

name: MindPortal
description: MindPortal develops advanced AI that decodes imagined speech into text using non‑invasive brain-computer interfaces. Their flagship model, MindSpeech, is the first to translate free-form thought into language, enabling hands-free, direct communication with AI and unlocking applications in healthcare, education, VR/AR, and beyond.

name: Minerva
description: Minerva Project partners with universities and governments to deliver active learning programs powered by its proprietary Forum platform. With a curriculum focused on critical thinking, global immersion, and real-world application, Minerva is helping institutions modernize higher education for a more connected and adaptable world.

name: MobLab
description: MobLab brings classrooms to life with engaging, mobile-first games that simulate economic principles, behavioral science, and strategic decision-making. Used in high schools and universities worldwide, the platform helps instructors deliver active learning experiences that deepen understanding through real-time participation and data analysis.

name: Mystery Science
description: Mystery Science provides open-and-go lessons that inspire kids to love science. The company's online resource makes it easy for elementary school teachers to deliver an incredible science lesson without a science background. Hands-on activities expose students to the joy of scientific inquiry at an early age.

name: Nerdy
description: Nerdy connects learners with expert instructors through its flagship platform, Varsity Tutors. Offering 1-on-1 tutoring, group classes, and adaptive self-study tools, Nerdy supports academic achievement, test prep, and professional development with a focus on personalization, convenience, and results.

name: NewGlobe
description: NewGlobe partners with governments to support learning gains at scale by providing schools with proven pedagogy, real-time data tools, and teacher coaching. Focused on foundational literacy and numeracy, NewGlobe helps improve outcomes in emerging markets through structured, high-impact instructional models.

name: Nexford
description: Nexford offers accredited undergraduate and graduate programs designed to align with workforce needs, focusing on business, technology, and entrepreneurship. With flexible pacing, transparent pricing, and a project-based curriculum, Nexford helps learners around the world build practical skills for the modern economy.

name: NoRedInk
description: NoRedInk helps students become stronger writers through personalized, standards-aligned exercises that adapt to their interests and skill levels. The platform guides learners through grammar, usage, and essay writing, while giving teachers real-time data to inform instruction and track growth over time.

name: NovoEd
description: NovoEd powers cohort-based courses that emphasize teamwork, practice, and feedback, helping organizations deliver high-impact learning at scale. From leadership development to onboarding, NovoEd enables companies and universities to create engaging, social learning experiences that drive real-world outcomes.

name: Ohai AI
description: Ohai is a groundbreaking personal AI assistant for parents, students, and households, allowing users to quickly check items off of their personal to-do list.

name: On Deck
description: On Deck connects aspiring and active entrepreneurs through structured fellowships, mentorship, and peer support. With curated programming, access to capital, and a global founder network, ODF helps individuals go from idea to startup with greater speed, clarity, and community.

name: Outschool
description: Outschool is an innovative education platform that offers a variety of engaging, small-group classes online. Unlike traditional classes or passive videos, Outschool gives kids the unique opportunity to explore their interests in-depth with interactive classes taught via live video by experienced, independent teachers and organizations.

name: Pathrise
description: Pathrise helps job seekers land roles in tech, business, and design through personalized coaching, interview prep, and job search strategy. With an income share model and outcomes-focused approach, Pathrise supports candidates from application to offer while aligning incentives around success.

name: Photomath
description: Photomath offers an award-winning AI-powered mobile app that makes math easy to understand and master. Its product features include instant solutions to scanned problems, step-by-step explanations, and engaging animated instruction for a wide range of math problems.

name: Polygence
description: Polygence pairs motivated high school students with PhD-level mentors to design and complete personalized research projects. Through one-on-one guidance and flexible scheduling, the platform supports academic exploration, portfolio building, and competitive college admissions.

name: Prenda
description: Prenda partners with parents and educators to launch small, local learning environments where students progress at their own pace. Combining online curriculum, project-based learning, and personal mentorship, Prenda offers a flexible, student-centered model that reimagines the traditional classroom.

name: Sakana AI
description: Sakana AI develops modular AI systems using principles from evolution and swarms. By combining smaller models into powerful composites, the company is reimagining how foundation models are designed, trained, and deployed for advanced reasoning and autonomy.

name: Scoutible
description: Scoutible is the world’s first game-based AI for hiring top talent, using psychometric profiling developed by expert psychologists to predict future performance of candidates. Its platform allows employers to find ideal employees for every position based on their essential qualities and provides job candidates an opportunity to showcase their talent via a quick-and-easy mobile game.

name: Section
description: Section helps organizations and professionals adopt AI effectively by combining strategic planning, tailored training, and workflow redesign. With tools like ProfAI — an on-demand AI coach— and enterprise-grade programs, Section enables teams to develop habits and capabilities needed to drive real ROI from AI.

name: Sharpen
description: Sharpen helps young children learn to read through a structured, dual-device program guided by parents. With phonics-based lessons and built-in progress tracking, the platform builds core literacy skills in just minutes a day.

name: Singularity University
description: Singularity University equips leaders with the knowledge and mindset to navigate rapid technological change. Through immersive learning, expert networks, and global summits, the platform helps individuals and organizations apply emerging tech to solve complex global challenges.

name: Smashcut
description: Smashcut delivers live, project-based courses with built-in video conferencing, feedback tools, and peer collaboration. Used by top institutions, the platform brings professional-grade media instruction to online classrooms.

name: SoloLearn
description: SoloLearn is the world’s largest community of mobile code learners, offering a freemium subscription product that allows users to learn different programming languages anytime, anywhere, and on any device. It features lessons, quizzes, discussion forums, and a global leaderboard that gamifies the learning experience.

name: Springboard
description: Springboard is a leading school for workforce upskilling that offers fully online, job guaranteed courses for early- and mid-career professionals in analytics, data science, design, and software engineering, among others. Students receive 1-on-1 weekly mentor sessions, with career advice and technical guidance.

name: Study Sync
description: StudySync offers a standards-aligned English Language Arts platform for grades 6–12 that blends classic texts with modern media. With video previews, reading supports, and writing scaffolds, StudySync engages diverse learners while giving teachers powerful tools to personalize instruction.

name: StudyEdge
description: Study Edge provides video-based tutoring, study guides, and adaptive practice tools to help students succeed in challenging courses. With platforms like College Success and Algebra Nation, Study Edge supports learning in core subjects through engaging, standards-aligned content.

name: Superintelligent
description: Superintelligent enables organizations to identify how AI agents can drive business impact and then connects them with vetted partners. Its Agent Readiness Audit uses intelligent voice agents to uncover opportunities, benchmark readiness, and recommend use cases. The resulting marketplace lets enterprises deploy tailored AI solutions with confidence.

name: Teachable
description: Teachable provides a platform that allows teachers to create and sell online courses on everything from programming, marketing and design to cake decorating, jazz guitar and watercolor painting. The solution features tools to build a course website and control branding, student data, and pricing all from a single dashboard.

name: Teachmint
description: Teachmint provides schools with an integrated platform for learning management, communication, content delivery, and administrative tools. Built to serve K–12 institutions in emerging markets, Teachmint helps educators digitize operations, improve classroom engagement, and scale access to quality instruction.

name: TrueMed
description: TrueMed enables wellness brands to accept HSA and FSA payments for science-backed products and services — from supplements and fitness gear to health tech — by integrating telehealth-based documentation into checkout. The platform empowers consumers to use pre-tax dollars for preventive health while expanding access for merchants and increasing order value.

name: Udemy
description: Udemy is an online learning and teaching content marketplace with on-demand courses from experts in different areas. It offers a consumer product for both learners (Udemy) and teachers (Teach on Udemy), as well as an enterprise product (Udemy for Business).

name: Uppercase
description: Uppercase Teach connects educators struggling with lesson planning, classroom management, or family engagement to vetted expert teachers or its AI coach, Casey. The platform delivers personalized, real-time guidance, peer community forums, and curated resources, empowering teachers to improve instruction with confidence and ease.

name: Upright Education
description: Upright partners with higher education institutions to offer job-focused training programs in tech, design, and business. With online instruction, mentorship, and career support, Upright helps adult learners transition into high-demand roles while expanding workforce development capacity for schools.

name: Verbling
description: Verbling connects learners with certified language teachers for 1-on-1 video lessons across dozens of languages. With flexible scheduling, integrated lesson tools, and native-speaking tutors, Verbling delivers personalized language instruction to learners around the world.

name: Wave Neuro
description: Wave Neuroscience combines EEG-based biomarker mapping with targeted neurostimulation to improve mood, focus, and cognitive health without pharmaceuticals or invasive procedures. Through its Braincare™ platform and MeRT® technology, the company delivers data-driven brain treatment protocols that are twice as effective as standard approaches.

name: Wonder Workshop
description: Wonder Workshop creates programmable robots and curriculum that teach coding, creativity, and problem-solving to students in grades K–8. With classroom-ready tools and cross-platform apps, the company helps schools make computer science engaging and accessible from an early age.

name: WriteLab
description: WriteLab used natural language processing to deliver real-time feedback on grammar, clarity, and style. Designed to help students strengthen their writing through iteration, the platform enabled educators to scale personalized support.

name: 10story
description: 10story designs engaging, hands-on math projects for grades K-8, where students learn and apply mathematical concepts through role-playing adventures. These collaborative problem-solving activities drive the story forward, transforming the classroom into a space for connection, friendship, joy, achievement, and deep learning.

name: 4th-Dimension Leaders
description: 4th-Dimension’s BUILD Fellowship centers the understanding that parents of color play an outsized role in the long-term outcomes of their children. This initiative addresses the opportunity gaps parents of color experience when navigating their children’s education by building their knowledge, abilities, and capacities to maneuver within complex educational systems, institutions, and networks. Fellows in this cohort program explore broad themes around legislation, power, influence, and direct action before designing and deploying a solution to a key challenge faced by their neighborhood school. Parents emerge from BUILD Fellowship empowered to organize and advocate for themselves, their students, and their community.

name: 7 Generation Games
