description: 7 Generation Games is developing 7 Gen Ed Blocks, a no-code platform that enables educators to create interactive, culturally responsive literacy learning experiences in multiple languages. Since its founding, the organization has been focused on building narrative and gaming experiences that close equity gaps and improve outcomes for indigenous students and multilingual learners. Now they are working to adapt existing code libraries (originally used to develop proprietary games) into modularized, easily editable code blocks that educators can customize through a digital platform. This effort represents an attempt to scale their impact by putting more power into the hands of educators and enabling them to bring their content to life in engaging and culturally responsive ways.

name: A+ Unlimited Potential (A+UP)
description: A+UP is a free public charter school serving secondary students in Houston’s Midtown District. The school offers a small, personalized learning experience for families seeking a supportive and challenging environment. A+UP helps all students grow as learners and individuals.

name: Academy of Visual and Performing Arts
description: Academy of Visual and Performing Arts (AVPA) is a creative arts public charter school, authorized by the Texas Education Agency, to serve students in Fort Worth, Texas. AVPA will open in 2023-2024 with 320 6-7 grade students. At capacity, AVPA will serve 1120 students in grades 6-12. AVPA is governed by Illumination Education, Inc., which holds the charter on behalf of the public. The Board of Directors consists of eight (8) diverse community members.

name: Acuarela Curriculum Cooperative
description: Acuarela Curriculum Cooperative is creating culturally sustaining, interdisciplinary bilingual curricula that interweaves and aligns to both literacy and rich content standards (science, social studies, arts), beginning in early childhood. We provide wraparound professional learning for teachers and leaders to support implementation of our comprehensive curriculum and build knowledge of the research behind its design, and we license our training as curricula for teacher preparation programs to better prepare novice teachers to deeply understand and provide high quality instruction as a lever for equity.

name: Admissions Gambit
description: Admissions Gambit aims to increase the number of underrepresented minorities and first-generation applicants to colleges that match their academic and extracurricular backgrounds. They support students and families by providing one-on-one guidance and support throughout the entire application process, given that many students lack sufficient access to school counselors, with the national average counselor to student ratio of 415-to-1.

name: Advance Rural Education Foundation
description: 2648 International Blvd Ste 301, Oakland, CA 94601  |    EIN: 94-3281780   |info@newschools.org|Accessibility Statement

name: AI for Equity
description: AI for Equity supports and convenes cohorts of school system leaders to leverage AI in service of a more equitable future for all students.

name: AI-Learners
description: AI-Learners is an edtech tool that helps students of all abilities learn early math and literacy through personalized games and analytics. It supports teachers by reducing time spent preparing accessible materials and provides comprehensive learning data insights for both parents and educators.

name: AIM – Academy for Innovation in Medicine
description: The Academy for Innovation in Medicine (AIM) is a STEMM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics, and Medicine) middle and high school launching in Fall 2026 to address Atlanta’s healthcare workforce shortage. AIM prepares students for high-demand healthcare careers.

name: Albuquerque Public Schools
description: 2648 International Blvd Ste 301, Oakland, CA 94601  |    EIN: 94-3281780   |info@newschools.org|Accessibility Statement

name: ALL in Education
description: ALL In Education ensures that communities most impacted by educational inequities lead decisions affecting all students. By engaging families and caregivers, the organization creates high-quality learning environments that drive multigenerational change and long-term opportunity.

name: All Means All
description: All Means All builds partnerships with school and system leaders to increase equitable outcomes and opportunities for students with disabilities and other intersectional identities that have been historically marginalized. Our flagship AMA School Leadership Program is a 15-month cohort-based fellowship for school leaders along with their school and/or network based teams that pairs repeated practicing of key adaptive skills (mindsets, adult culture and problem solving) and the technical levers to address inequity in schools with the deep coaching, feedback and collaboration to sustain it. Leaders in the program will work to shrink key academic and/or social emotional opportunity gaps rooted in systemic racism and ableism for students with disabilities with a particular focus on Black and Latino students from low-income backgrounds.

name: American Indian Science and Engineering Society (AISES)
description: The American Indian Science and Engineering Society (AISES) is a national nonprofit organization focused on substantially increasing the representation of Indigenous peoples of North America and the Pacific Islands in science, technology, engineering, and math (STEM) studies and careers. AISES is launching the The Indigenous Knowledge to Grow Successful STEM Teachers initiative to address the lack of Native STEM teachers in education. This initiative will target three distinct prospective teacher populations including: Indigenous STEM professionals interested in changing careers, Native secondary and college students interested in STEM education careers, and Indigenous paraprofessionals looking to become certified teachers.

name: ARCADIA SCHOOLS
description: Arcadia High School provides a collaborative, student-centered learning environment where team teaching, personalized support, and ongoing teacher development prepare every student for success in college, career, and community.

name: Aventura Community School
description: Aventura Community School is a public school that launched in Nashville, TN in Fall 2022. The school intends to serve 930 students at full enrollment across grades K-8. The mission of Aventura Community School to elevate peace and justice by inspiring our diverse community to live as curious global citizens dedicated to changing the world. Following their K-8 journey at Aventura Community School, students will graduate with academic mastery, global competence including biliteracy in English and Spanish, and a track record of positive contribution to their community that together ensure they are prepared for success in high school, college, and a meaningful life

name: Barbershop Books
description: Reading So Lit, developed by Barbershop Books, uses social-emotional learning and multimedia to help preK-5 learners build vocabulary and express their reading preferences. This AI-powered platform delivers meaningful reading experiences, while strengths-based assessments and actionable data help educators personalize instruction and accelerate learning through student-driven decisions.

name: Bedford Stuyvesant New Beginnings Charter Schools
description: At Bedford Stuyvesant New Beginnings Charter Schools, families, educators, and community members collaborate to create a supportive and academically rigorous environment. Combining 21st-century learning, project-based and service learning, and traditional coursework, New Beginnings prepares students to thrive in competitive academic settings and to become responsible, engaged global citizens.

name: BeeReaders
description: BeeReaders Español offers culturally responsive content, adaptive assessments, and educator/family supports to build Spanish-speaking students’ literacy skills in both Spanish and English. Research has shown that developing these students’ Spanish literacy is beneficial not only to their development of English reading and writing skills, but also to a positive sense of identity and belonging. While the solution was developed for use in Latin American countries, the team is now prioritizing growth in dual language and bilingual programs in the United States.

name: BELIEVE Schools, Inc
description: BELIEVE Circle City High School is a public college and career preparatory high school dedicated to the achievement of students in the Indianapolis area. Our mission is to develop students into leaders through a community that fosters their agency, autonomy, and acceleration— providing a foundation for future independence and success.

name: BELIEVE St. Louis
description: BELIEVE Academy is a public college and career preparatory high school focused on student achievement in the St. Louis area. The school’s mission is to develop students into leaders through a community that emphasizes agency, autonomy, and acceleration, laying the foundation for independence and long-term success.

name: Bernalillo Public Schools
description: Bernalillo Public Schools seeks to reduce the barriers of entry and increase the long-term retention of Indigenous and Hispanic educators by offering a combination of financial, educational, and instructional support in their Diverse Educator Fellowship.

name: Bilingual Generation
description: Bili by Bilingual Generation is a bilingual learning app, approved by both science and “abuelita.” Designed for children ages 3-8, Bili offers culturally responsive learning experiences to address the digital tools gap for over 11 million young emergent multilingual learners in the U.S.

name: Black Education for New Orleans (BE NOLA)
description: BE NOLA is launching the Black is Brilliant Institute. The Black is Brilliant Institute is a six month paid fellowship for New Orleans’ Black teachers that provides a wellness and learning space. This fellowship is rooted in educators’ and students’ community cultural wealth and is dedicated to teachers’ pursuit of preparation, wholeness, and liberation. The Institute elevates educators’ connections to their Community Cultural Wealth and develops their ability to teach content that is connected to the “ground under their students’ feet.” The Institute offers a fusion of holistic development previously unavailable to Black teachers in New Orleans.

name: Black Educator Advocates Network
description: The Black Educators Advocates Network exists to transform the environment, practices, and policies that affect the educational experience for Black educators, administrators, students, and parents. Founded by Black educators who recognized the desperate need for an educational experience that considers and centers around Black students, BEAN is committed to transforming the learning environment for students of color. BEAN centers its efforts around community building, research and discovery, and bold advocacy.

name: BrainScience Works
description: BrainScience Works envisions a future where neuroscience, technology, and innovative teaching methods make literacy accessible to all. Their programs build confidence and success for struggling readers, unlocking the potential of every learner through transformative, inclusive education.

name: Bridge 2 Hope St. Louis Parent Institute
description: Bridge 2 Hope St. Louis is a parent-led organization with a mission to educate, empower and activate parents and grandparents to disrupt the system of inequity, fight unjust policies, and advocate for their children’s right to quality educational options. Bridge 2 Hope St. Louis empowers parents and their children through intentional outreach to the most vulnerable communities with a focus on on building strong communal relationships.

name: Bridgemakers
description: Bridgemakers is building a national movement of Black, Latino and low-income student changemakers to help drive the purpose-driven dramatic improvement of American public secondary schooling through youth-led public leadership and learning. We advance this vision by building school-based student changing-making and school-connected learning labs.

name: Brothers Liberating Our Communities
description: Brothers Liberating Our Communities works to increase the number of Black Male Educators in schools. Through retention and sustainment strategies, BLOC hopes that all students will have access to dynamic Black men in the educational experience. The Akoma Ntoso Fellowship supports, trains. and retains early and mid-career Black male educators in Kansas City by curating safe, liberatory spaces.

name: Build Our Lives Together Inc
description: Building Our Lives Together, Inc (BOLT) aims to redistribute resources to strengthen people power in marginalized communities, by providing leadership development, organizing, and advocacy programming for high school students, equipping them with the tools, resources, and network to lead change. Their work takes root in Philadelphia, where economic disparities are persistent and continue to have an outsized impact on students in marginalized communities.BOLT’s long-term vision is to build the next generation of powerful grassroots youth leaders from marginalized neighborhoods who will go on to build thriving neighborhoods and inspire others from their communities to do the same.

name: Butler Academy
description: Butler Academy is a tuition-free K-12 public charter school in Darlington County, SC that launched in Fall 2022. The school intends to serve 170 students at full enrollment across grades 6-8. Their mission is to create an enthusiastic culture of learning in which every scholar is expected and positioned to succeed academically. Employing a Compassionate School model, we realize our mission by emphasizing the social and emotional well-being of our scholars, families, and staff. Their BRAVE core values (Belonging, Respect, Accountability, Vision, and Excellence) inform and guide all aspects of our work.

name: California Indian Museum and Cultural Center
description: The purpose of the California Indian Museum and Cultural Center is to culturally enrich and benefit the people of California and the general public. The goals of CIMCC are to educate the public about California Indian history and cultures, from a native perspective.

name: Camelback Ventures
description: Camelback Ventures increases access to opportunity for entrepreneurs of color and women by investing in their ventures and leadership while advocating for fairness in their funding. Camelback is building thriving communities powered by diverse social innovators in education and technology.

name: Capti – Charmtech Labs LLC
description: Capti is a literacy platform that helps improve reading for students of all abilities with evidence based solutions. The new initiative will empower researchers to prototype experimental reading interventions, validate them, and bring them to market via Capti. Capti will democratize intervention development, shorten time to market, and enable researchers to scale their impact.

name: Celebrate Dyslexia
description: Celebrate Dyslexia is on a mission to celebrate, educate and empower the one in five with dyslexia. Our vision is every human with dyslexia is equitably prepared to live, work and experience life with limitless opportunity. Overall, Celebrate Dyslexia’s multi-faceted approach, engaging partnerships across various sectors, and addressing different aspects of dyslexia education and support demonstrate a commitment to achieving a dyslexia-aware environment.

name: Celebrate EDU
description: Celebrate EDU provides individuals with disabilities the skills needed to become entrepreneurs. Celebrate EDU’snew initiative will provide curricula to create group-based microenterprises for youth with learning differences in high school. The goal is to introduce entrepreneurship to students and encourage leadership development through business education.

name: Central Brooklyn Literacy Academy
description: Literacy Academy Collective (LAC) designs and supports NYC public schools in implementing evidence-based literacy instruction. By partnering with schools, families, community-based organizations, and higher education institutions, LAC delivers culturally relevant, structured literacy to marginalized students at the intersection of race, income, and disability.

name: Central Queens Academy
description: Central Queens Academy Charter Schools operate two schools in the heart of Queens, New York City, serving a diverse community of scholars. As the schools expand to enroll 1,800 students in grades K-8, they aim to equip students with the skills and knowledge needed to excel in education, thrive in the workforce, and contribute meaningfully to their communities.

name: ChattAcademy Community School
description: In partnership with families, ChattAcademy Community School empowers students in grades 6-12 to explore their curiosity, build global competence through bilingual studies, make a positive impact on their communities, and develop strong character. The school prepares students to excel in the postsecondary opportunities of their choice.

name: Childhood Education International (CE International)
description: Childhood Education International (CE International) is committed to developing and amplifying innovative solutions to education challenges. CE International is launching the Refugee Educator Academy, an initiative dedicated to supporting prospective educators of color living in displacement, conflict, crisis, migration, and resettlement contexts. The Refugee Educator Academy will build a structured, supportive, and responsive pathway to teaching for people of refugee/immigrant backgrounds. In facilitated communities of practice, participants will follow a year-long journey that includes learning, micro-credentialing, mentorship, career exploration, and creation of personalized plans to reach career goals. This initiative will diversify the teacher workforce, address teacher shortages, improve education quality, and create more inclusive, culturally relevant schools.

name: CitySchools Collaborative
description: CitySchools Collaborative catalyzes and scales school-based interventions. With support from NewSchools, it is developing Sophia, an AI tutor coach aligned to its proprietary math curriculum that provides tutors with real-time support to deliver stronger instruction.

name: Code Switch Restorative Justice for Girls of Color
description: Code Switch Restorative Justice for Girls of Color (Code Switch) seeks to eradicate the inequities shaping the K-12 experiences of Black girls. Based on the belief that Black girls thrive when they feel seen, heard, and valued, Code Switch envisions K-12 schools as spaces where Black girls are free to be and become their authentic selves, are empowered to engage their unique ways of being, and are encouraged to imagine a future for themselves beyond the worlds they see before them.

name: Cognitive ToyBox
description: Cognitive ToyBox develops game-based tools that make early childhood assessment efficient, actionable and equitable. Their mission is to help every child start school ready to succeed. Through the Learning Differences initiative, they are developing new research-based tools to support the assessment of social-emotional and executive functioning skills for 3-to-5 year old children.

name: CommunityShare
description: CommunityShare ignites civic engagement and a passion for learning by tapping into the wisdom and lived experiences of educators, students, and community members. Through collaboration, CommunityShare co-creates real-world learning experiences that build relationships, deepen understanding, and empower collective action.

name: Convivir Colorado
description: 2648 International Blvd Ste 301, Oakland, CA 94601  |    EIN: 94-3281780   |info@newschools.org|Accessibility Statement

name: Coursemojo Inc.
description: Coursemojo empowers educators to meet the diverse needs of all students by providing accessible, interactive, and inclusive instructional materials. With Mojo, an AI-powered virtual teaching assistant, Coursemojo offers students personalized 1:1 support on curriculum-aligned activities.

name: Cultivating Literacy
description: Cultivating Literacy partners with schools to support dual-language TK-3rd grade students and their families by providing individualized reading support to improve accuracy and confidence. With the help of a bilingual reading coach and digital family portal, students engage in research-based reading activities that make learning enjoyable and strengthen the connection between school and home.

name: Da Vinci Schools
description: Da Vinci Schools operates six highly diverse schools in LA serving 2700+ students from 126 zip codes. We incorporate college/career readiness and project-based learning, LifeLaunch will blend high school, college credits, and career training focused on students aged 16-20. We envision a regional K-12 partnership across several districts, businesses and higher education, eliminating hurdles and stigmas often faced by the most marginalized learners.

name: Da Vinci Schools, LifeLaunch
description: Da Vinci Schools operates six highly diverse schools in LA serving 2700+ students from 126 zip codes. We incorporate college/career readiness and project-based learning, LifeLaunch will blend high school, college credits, and career training focused on students aged 16-20. We envision a regional K-12 partnership across several districts, businesses and higher education, eliminating hurdles and stigmas often faced by the most marginalized learners.

name: Data Research for Indigenous Futures (DRIF)
description: Data Research for Indigenous futures (DRIF): Our vision is creating Indigenous futures by collaboratively working to uphold Indigenous data sovereignty so that Native children and youth are provided with life-changing possibilities and support to thrive in educational school systems across Indigenous North America.

name: DC Public Education Fund
description: The DC Public Education Fund was founded in 2007 to help DC Public Schools address its most pressing challenges through organized philanthropy. As an independent organization, our team raises significant private dollars that incubate cutting-edge start-up ideas for students. To combat the lack of teacher engagement and a dearth of culturally affirming foundational literacy resources, through Readers Next Door DCPS trained their very own DCPS educators to create culturally relevant decodable books for students in grades K-2.

name: Diffit
description: Diffit helps teachers to instantly get differentiated lesson resources for any topic, article, or reading. This saves teachers tons of time – while helping all students, regardless of reading level, to access grade level curriculum.

name: Diverse Learners Cooperative
description: The Diverse Learners Cooperative will launch an 18-month, job-embedded special education certification program to increase the number of qualified special education teachers in local schools. In the first year, it will support a cohort of first-year special educators by providing job-embedded support and gathering targeted feedback to create responsive curricula and supportive certification pathways.

name: Diversity in Leadership Institute
description: The Diversity in Leadership Institute, a nonprofit 501(c)3 organization, identifies, develops, and supports racially diverse public school leaders to serve as change agents to close the opportunity gap, through movement building, growing talent pipelines for excellence in school leadership and advocacy.

name: Duquesne City School District
description: Offer rigorous & personalized learning, foster academic achievement, and support social-emotional development and career readiness through community partnerships, ultimately empowering excellence and growth for adults and student

name: Dyslexia Alliance for Black Children
description: Dyslexia Alliance for Black Children works to eliminate the amplified inequities for Black children experiencing unaddressed dyslexia or related learning disabilities. Through collaborative efforts with families and educators, we inform, empower, and raise awareness of the need for early identification and intervention for Black children with dyslexia through sharing knowledge, dismantling systems of bias, and paving pathways to evidence-based reading instruction.

name: EarlyBird Education
description: EarlyBird is a game-based assessment that screens for dyslexia as well as moderate reading risk that can be addressed through multi-tiered systems of support. Based on research conducted by the co-founders at Boston Children’s Hospital, Harvard Graduate School of Education, and the FSU-Florida Center for Reading Research, it can accurately identify students who might benefit from specialized support related to foundational reading skills. Such “early identification” practices are a key lever for supporting long-term literacy and health outcomes.

name: Earsight
description: Earsight builds tools to support struggling readers achieve automaticity in their reading.

name: Edge Fearless Learning
description: Edge is an intensive intervention in literacy and math – virtually tutoring students with dyslexia-effects and developing an open learning management solution for dyscalculia and dyscalculia effects. Edge seeks to influence how children learn by also training teachers in the Science of Math.

name: Edgecombe County Public Schools
description: Promote a community of learning that gives our students an “EDGE” in pursuit of the graduate aims. All scholars will graduate prepared to design their own futures, navigate change, and make the world a better place

name: EdLight
description: EdLight bridges educational gaps by building technology that connects teachers and learners. With a mission rooted in equity, EdLight empowers Black and Brown students to reach their full potential by providing educators with AI-driven tools that make instruction more student-centered.

name: Education Evolving (Teacher Powered Schools)
description: The Teacher-Powered Coaching Initiative provides services for teams seeking to transform teacher roles and improve student outcomes using a teacher-powered model. The initiative supports educators in collaboratively designing and running schools, addressing key issues like working conditions, professional autonomy, and respect, enabling them to do their best work for students.

name: Education Partnership of the Permian Basin
description: The Education Partnership of the Permian Basin in Texas is dedicated to improving educational outcomes across the region, supporting students from cradle to career.

name: Education Reimagined
description: Education Reimagined is a national non-profit dedicated to catalyzing the invention of a new public education system; one where the education system becomes the backbone to dynamic, interdependent ecosystems of learning that can uniquely, powerfully, and equitably serve, love, and include every child in the country.

name: Edunomics Lab Georgetown University
description: Edunomics Lab is a Georgetown University-based research center dedicated to exploring and modeling complex education finance decisions to inform policy and practice. The center provides timely, relevant and actionable analysis of education finance policy and resource allocation strategy and decisions. As a response to the ESSER dollars, Edunomics Lab established a professional development and training experience tailored to school board members. The experience offers financial training to representatives from the communities that were the hardest hit from the COVID-19 pandemic.

name: edwell
description: Edwell is on a mission to ensure every teacher has the knowledge, skills, and resources to meet the mental health needs of their students and themselves– the two pieces of the school climate puzzle. They believe that to teach the whole student, you also need to support the whole teacher.

name: Elevate 215
description: Elevate 215’s Citywide Talent Coalition aims to recruit, retain, and build high-quality educators of color in Philadelphia. This coalition consists of invested partners, across a diverse range of sectors, dedicated to implementing the Philadelphia’s Citywide Strategy for Diverse and High-Quality Teachers. In collaboration with the Coalition, Elevate 215 will lead coordinating efforts to define teacher quality, track progress towards goals, and collect data to inform future strategy and continuous improvement efforts.

name: Ella Baker Institute
description: Ella Baker Institute’s mission is to provide community-responsive learning experiences where young people lead and communities gain the holistic supports they need to thrive as radical changemakers in their communities and beyond. Centering our VOICE framework to drive equity and impact, our programming and offerings center on Community Well-Being, Youth Leadership and Organizational Well-Being. Our flagship initiative is the Young People’s Leadership Cooperative where young people engage youth-led participatory action research (YPAR) to identify challenges in their community, investigate those challenges and take action to generate greater community well-being.

name: Elm Community Charter School
description: Elm aims to create a community space dedicated to innovation, student-led creation, and academic rigor. Our mission is to cultivate student leaders with the character and capacity to define their own identity, seize opportunity, and shape the world around them.

name: Emote Education Inc
description: Emote’s Care Copilot uses AI to expand educators’ capacity, turning real-time data into actionable insights for proactive student support. The platform empowers all staff — not just specialists — to identify and address the unique needs of every learner, especially those with learning differences.

name: Encompass Community School
description: Encompass Community is a proposed K-8 public school in Nashville offering a mastery-based, student-centered approach to education. The school is dedicated to ensuring all students thrive both academically and personally.

name: Endazhi-Nitaawiging “The Place Where it Grows”
description: Endazhi-Nitaawiging is a public school that launched in Red Lake, Minnesota in Fall 2022. The school intends to serve 150 students at full enrollment across grades K-8.At the heart of Ojibwe culture is the connection between individuals and Mother Earth. The Red Lake Charter School aims to strengthen the Red Lake Nation by providing our children with an education grounded in Ojibwe values that is academically rigorous and celebrates Indigenous culture. Endazhi-Nitaawiging “The Place Where it Grows” mission is to prepare each student for college with an enhanced knowledge of the Ojibwe language, culture, leadership, and environmental stewardship.

name: Enlighten AI, Inc.
description: Enlighten AI builds AI tools for educators to supercharge their impact while drastically reducing burnout. Our first product is an AI grading and feedback assistant for teachers, enabling faster, better teacher feedback personalized to each teacher’s style, expectations, and voice.

name: Ethos Classical
description: 2648 International Blvd Ste 301, Oakland, CA 94601  |    EIN: 94-3281780   |info@newschools.org|Accessibility Statement

name: Excellence in Education, Inc.
description: Their mission is to provide every student — especially those historically underserved — with an education grounded in equity, personalization, and mastery of essential skills. We create learning environments where every child can excel when given the right time, tools, and support. With high expectations and strong support, we prepare students to thrive academically, socially, and personally as resilient lifelong learners and leaders, ready for college, career, and life.

name: Excelsior Village Academies, Inc.
description: Excelsior Village Academies is a public, tuition-free K-8 classical education charter school, co-designed with the community to promote service, impact, and equity in education.

name: Expert IEP
description: Expert IEP’s goal is to empower caregivers of youth who think and learn differently to participate as valued members of the IEP team. We achieve their goal by leveraging machine learning to elevate caregiver assets, help caregivers advocate for clear and appropriate IEP goals, and monitor service delivery as well as overall student progress. We are the first IEP optimization app for families by families.

name: Fair Schools
description: Fair Schools supports school leaders in creating thriving learning environments for students of diverse backgrounds and needs.

name: Family Engagement Lab
description: Family Engagement Lab was part of the Early Learning Ed Tech Challenge. Funds supported Family Engagement Lab’s digital products to help schools ignite the power of family engagement.

name: Felix Commonwealth Virtual School
description: The Felix Commonwealth Virtual School combines a competency-based digital learning platform with in-person learning options at community-based sites. The school emphasizes flexible learning and community connections to prepare students to thrive in a changing world, empowering them to learn at their own pace and reach their full potential.

name: First Tech Fund, Inc
description: First Tech Fund is dedicated to empowering low-income high school students in identifying, navigating, and attaining academic and professional opportunities through a supportive, adaptive ecosystem where they get access to free technology, skills training, social capital, mentorship, and more.

name: FLi Sci
description: FLi Sci supports students furthest from opportunity by providing them with early, formative experiences necessary to become the next generation of scientists from historically excluded backgrounds. Our flagship program, FLi Sci Scholars, targets students from schools or areas with limited science opportunities and provide them with foundational research experiences to help them access science careers. FLi Sci aims to dramatically diversify scientific professions to ensure that our leading researchers better reflect the demographics of the populations impacted by scientific, medical, and technological breakthroughs.

name: Forged Ed LLC
description: Forged Ed’s new initiative an identity-affirming, culturally-responsive, engaging SEL program that directly centers the needs and experiences of students of color designed through collaboration with young people of color. Our advisory curriculum is accompanied by our high-quality professional development workshops to support educators in developing the knowledge, mindsets and skills needed for its implementation.

name: Fort Worth STEAM Academy
description: Fort Worth STEAM Academy is a public charter middle school opening in August 2026. Our mission is to prepare every student for success in high school, college, and career through a STEAM-focused education, a joyful culture of high expectations, and strong community partnerships.

name: Forte Preparatory Academy
description: Forte Prep is a tuition-free middle school open to students in grades 5 through 8 in Queens, New York. We are driven by a philosophy of achieving excellence in all facets of life and learning. We believe in the power of education to transform the lives of students from diverse, often disadvantaged, backgrounds. Through rigorous academics, creative & technological enrichment and leadership development, we prepare our students to excel at college preparatory high schools and beyond.

name: ForwardEd
description: ForwardEd creatively expands and diversifies the teacher candidate pipeline for Los Angeles schools by assisting emergency credentialed teachers and the school-based teams that support them to ensure teachers reach the next credentialing level needed to become fully certified.

name: Foust Gaming and Robotics School
description: 2648 International Blvd Ste 301, Oakland, CA 94601  |    EIN: 94-3281780   |info@newschools.org|Accessibility Statement

name: FULCRUM
description: FULCRUM is a national nonprofit working to end illiteracy by advancing evidence-based reading instruction in schools, educator preparation programs, and policy — ensuring all children learn to read. We believe literacy is the fundamental civil right of our time.

name: Georgia Educators for Equity and Justice
description: Georgia Educators for Equity and Justice Incorporated is a professional organization which champions the liberation and belonging of Black educators, students, and educational stakeholders. We endeavor to extend resources and guidance in a variety of areas including, but not limited to: advocacy, charitable purposes, civic engagement and responsibility, professional development, as well as recruitment and retention.

name: Gladeo Inc.
description: FutureReady for All is an initiative to revolutionize postsecondary readiness, career development, and independent living skills for students with IEPs and intellectual and developmental disabilities. In partnership with CAST, Think College, and DC Special Ed, the program introduces a pioneering digital curriculum, integrates UDL principles, and features the “Role Models Video Project” to inspire accessibility for all learners.

name: Goblins
description: Goblins provides schools with 1:1 math support for every student. Learners can speak aloud or hand-draw their thinking, and the platform identifies the “why” behind confusion — building conceptual understanding in real time.

name: Greater Atlanta Preparatory School
description: Greater Atlanta Preparatory School (GAPS) is an all-boys public charter school opening in 2027 to serve South Metro Atlanta. Rooted in character, leadership, education, arts, and resilience, GAPS prepares young men to thrive in college, career, and life.

name: Green Schools National Network, Inc.
description: Green Schools National Network’s mission is to create schools that are healthy, equitable, and sustainable. GSNN partners with schools for 5 years to produce and implement a strategic plan for whole-school and whole-district change in the areas of leadership, culture / climate, curriculum / instruction, and facilities. Started as a network of affiliated schools in 2007, GSNN relaunched in 2021 with a more structured GreenPrint 2.0 to provide whole-school model supports for their network schools. Curriculum and instruction in GSNN schools use phenomena-, place-, project-, and problem-based learning to increase student engagement, deepen learning, and breathe life into standards. This cross-curricular approach allows students to build knowledge, skills, and mindsets for taking action in their schools and communities.

name: Grokkoli PBC
description: Grokkoli is a FERPA-compliant AI math tutor that helps students with special needs—including those with dyscalculia and cognitive impairments—advance multiple grade levels in a single school year. Designed for grades K–5, it focuses on the foundational years where math challenges often begin.

name: Health Education And Learning Highschool
description: 2648 International Blvd Ste 301, Oakland, CA 94601  |    EIN: 94-3281780   |info@newschools.org|Accessibility Statement

name: High School for Recording Arts
description: The mission of HSRA is to provide youth the opportunity to achieve a high school diploma through the exploration and operation of the music business and other creative endeavors.

name: Hola Books
description: Hola Books is a Spanish educational publisher creating authentic, research-based decodable books and resources that help bilingual children learn to read confidently in two or more languages.

name: Honor Prep Charter School
description: Honor Preparatory Charter School delivers equitable instruction using a dual language model that empowers scholars to be self advocates, ensuring success in middle school and beyond. Our school community is designed in partnership with the community and guided by principle that all families deserve choice in their education.

name: I Dream Big Academy
description: I Dream Big Academy is an HBCU Charter located on the historic campus of Stillman College. Its mission is to provide a rigorous and relevant learning environment rooted in meaningful relationships that prepares students to pursue their passions and purposeful careers while becoming the best version of themselves.

name: IEP&Me
description: IEP&Me is reimagining specialized education for students, families, and educators through a simplified and digestible platform, encouraging collaboration and providing resources that promote self-advocacy and transparency.

name: Illinois Virtual Academy
description: Illinois Virtual Academy will launch as a virtual public school to serve students across Illinois in Fall 2023. The school intends to serve 10,000 students at full enrollment across grades K-12. Illinois Virtual Academy (IVA) is based on a new vision for engaged virtual and remote learning with an emphasis on applied and project-based options. IVA provides an immersive and supportive learning environment where students collaborate, create, and explore. We go “beyond the online course” to provide a connected, student-centered experience.

name: ILO Group
description: The ILO Group is a women-owned education strategy and policy firm that supports school districts and states in executing on their biggest bets and their highest priority efforts. The ILO Group is launching Women Leading Ed (WLE), a national network committed to supporting and advancing women, especially women of color, in education leadership through research, advocacy, and engagement.

name: Impactful Projects, Inc
description: Through the Impactful PBL Network, we strengthen educators’ capacity to implement equity-focused instructional practices via project-based learning. We provide the training, resources, and community necessary for teachers to design and facilitate memorable project-based learning experiences that empower students to tackle challenges in their school and community.

name: Independence Preparatory Academy
description: Independence Preparatory Academy provides a supportive and challenging environment that fosters academic excellence, personal growth, and lifelong learning. The school emphasizes critical thinking, creativity, and collaboration in its curriculum to prepare students for success in a rapidly changing world.

name: Indigitize
description: Indigitize activates and equips Indigenous youth, families, and educators with the literacy and tools to shape emerging technologies in service of their communities.

name: Innova Academy Inc.
description: Innova Academy empowers its learning community through an education model centered on the power of play. By weaving play into every aspect of learning, the school sparks curiosity, nurtures creativity, and inspires a lifelong love of learning.

name: InnovateEDU
description: InnovateEdu’s Pathways Alliance initiative is building a national coalition to cultivate the policy, practice, and research conditions necessary to build and sustain a diverse and inclusive pipeline of educators. Formed in response to the challenges faced by prospective educators of color, the Pathways Alliance works to make education an accessible, sustainable, and inclusive profession for all.

name: Innovation For Equity
description: Innovation For Equity (IFE) is a network of Black changemakers across the education ecosystem – leaders in PK-12, postsecondary, research, technology, and business – committed to disrupting the status quo and transforming the life outcomes of Black learners of all ages. By connecting and cultivating Black leaders in education to build collective power and scale innovative solutions, IFE reimagines a future where Black learners thrive across the nation.

name: Intensified Accelerated Systems, Inc.
description: Building on years of highly effective, standalone, literacy-based video courses, Intensified Accelerated Systems has created Student Academic Mastery Performances (SAMP), an initiative that cultivates school and community partnerships to advance Black students’ early literacy skills. Teachers, community groups and families leverage original, online videos to support students in developing foundational literacy skills at school, at community centers including churches, and at home. Literacy skills are reinforced in the different spaces of a student’s life. The culmination of students’ learning results in a school performance (in a classroom) and community performance (in a community center).

name: Intersection Collective
description: The Intersection Collective is guided by equity, inclusion, accessibility, and belonging. It focuses on Black, Indigenous, and Latinx communities with disabilities, who face the greatest disparities. The organization amplifies, learns from, and values the lived experiences of children and families at the intersection of race and disability.

name: Invictus Nashville Charter School
description: Invictus Nashville Charter School is will cultivate college and career ready young adults with a diverse worldview that will become leaders in their communities. In partnerships with families, we will will prepare our K–8 students to become engaged members of society by helping them identify their unique path to personal and professional success. We will do this through a diverse culture, personalized learning, community service, and equipping our students with the necessary habits of success and a heart for service in their communities.

name: Jackson Museum School
description: Jackson Museum School is a free, public charter school serving grades K–6. Its mission is to cultivate an innovative, student-centered environment that prioritizes academic excellence and character development.

name: Jounce Partners
description: Jounce Partners’ Expertise Leadership Opportunity Project (ELOP) recruits, hires, and develops Black and Latino school leaders. This initiative addresses barriers experienced by Black and Latino prospective school leaders, including the tendency to direct leaders of color toward non-instructional leadership positions. The ELOP initiative increases representation in school leadership roles and helps Black and Latino leaders operate with deep expertise in the science of how children learn.

name: Journalistic Learning Initiative
description: The Journalistic Learning Initiative has launched Effective Communicators, a course for underserved rural students of color that improves written and oral communication skills. Educators participate in a teaching fellowship that trains them to deliver and tailor the curriculum to their respective classrooms. Students are able to go through the course and select topics of interest to research, write, and produce stories about, learning how to distinguish fact from fiction along the way.

name: Journify Learning
description: Journify Learning is an evidence-based AI assistant that maximizes the efficiency and effectiveness of special education teams. It saves educators time and improves student outcomes through proven practices, automating progress tracking and reporting.

name: Joy Education Foundation
description: Joy Education is a technology-nonprofit that reduces inequities in access to affordable literacy support in low-income communities. We operate virtual reading clinics to help students overcome reading difficulty by providing a digital diagnostic, on-demand digital curriculum grounded in the Science of Reading, and one-to-one online tutoring in reading, comprehension and spelling for students.

name: KConnect
description: KConnect is a network of public and private organizations working to advance a collective movement aimed at ensuring all children in Kent County have an equitable path to economic prosperity through quality education, family, and community support. KConnect’s Future Educators of Color Network will create equitable pathways for children to access quality education and community support by developing a new coalition addressing the deep need for diverse teachers within Kent County, MI.

name: Kindle Education Public Charter School
description: Kindle Education is an intentionally diverse 6th-12th grade public charter school in Jersey City, NJ that prioritizes holistic, self-directed learning. Our mission is to cultivate an equitable community of purposeful individuals who are prepared to navigate our current world and build our future.

name: Kindle Education Public Charter School
description: Kindle Education is an intentionally diverse 6th-12th grade public charter school in Jersey City, NJ that will launch in Fall 2023. The school will prioritize holistic, self-directed learning. Their mission is to cultivate an equitable community of purposeful individuals who are prepared to navigate our current world and build our future.

name: Kindred Communities
description: Kindred Communities’ Advocacy Program supports community-led educational change by engaging community advocates of color, both educators and caregivers, in a civil discourse on race and equity in  community schools. In this initiative, community advocates learn about the local political landscape, city decision-making processes, and deepen their understanding of citywide systems of privilege and marginalization. Advocates develop the skills necessary to engage in formal advocacy, including participating in the testimony process at the city level. This initiative cultivates a pipeline of advocates ready to collaborate with policymakers, school system leaders, and other decision-makers to create sustainable movement towards educational fairness and justice for all students.

name: Koalluh, Inc.
description: LitLab.ai is the world’s first “AI for early reading” that lets K-2 teachers produce decodable content for early readers.

name: Kwenda Collegiate Girls Charter School
description: Kwenda Collegiate Girls is a feminist, tuition-free K-5 public charter school in Brooklyn, New York, dedicated to educating girls and gender-expansive youth. Kwenda reimagines schooling to provide all girls with access to an excellent education that empowers them to lead self-determined lives filled with promise, wonder, and ease. The school cultivates a joyful, inclusive environment alongside rigorous academics to prepare students to be the changemakers our world needs.

name: Latino Education Advancement Foundation (LEAF)
description: Latino Education Advancement Foundation’s (LEAF) mission is to improve outcomes for Latino students and families in East San José by providing college enrollment and persistence support, student and family career preparation assistance, and educational equity and systemic change advocacy. LEAF’s Familias Unidas del Este de San José  Fellowship (FUES) is builds local Latino families’ capacities and abilities to advocate for equitable funding for East San José’s K-12 schools. FUES is built on the belief that every low-income, Latino student is entitled to an equal opportunity to succeed in higher education and pursue the career of their choice to obtain social and economic mobility.

name: Latino Educational Equity Partnerships (LEEP)
description: LEEP’s mission is to increase the number of effective Latino school leaders in dual language education. LEEP provides world-class training and mentorship to passionate Latino prospective dual-language school leaders committed to cultivating culturally-affirming K-12 learning in two languages. LEEP is seeks to establish schools that bridge both linguistic and cultural barriers.

name: Latinos for Education
description: Latinos for Education develops, places, and connects leaders in the education sector while advancing policies and practices that remove systemic barriers to equity. With a decade of experience serving families, educators, and leaders, we are building a powerful movement to elevate Latino voices.

name: Lead Collaborative
description: Lead Collaborative’s mission is to build the capacity of educational leaders within the system, their schools and their communities by equipping them with the tools needed to improve student outcomes.

name: Lead Liberated
description: Through healing and learning, Lead Liberated equips leaders in education to interrupt racism within themselves and their organizations in order to co-design antiracist learning cultures.

name: LEAD: Leaders of Excellence, Advocacy, and Discovery
description: 2648 International Blvd Ste 301, Oakland, CA 94601  |    EIN: 94-3281780   |info@newschools.org|Accessibility Statement

name: Lectores para el futuro
description: Our organization focuses on evidence based explicit reading instruction and interventions for students with reading difficulties. We aim to transform the way reading is taught to unlock the full potential of all students, especially struggling readers and to provide professional development to educators.

name: Liberation Journeys
description: Liberation Journeys partners with district and school leaders to build anti-racist middle and high schools that center student voice in the design process. There are three components of the program. First is Liberation Leaders, a two-year student leadership development institute that develops the next generation of racial equity leaders. Second is an anti-racist curriculum that centers students’ social emotional development and untapped design skills with a literacy component. Finally, Liberation Journeys offers training for school leaders and teachers on how to co-design liberatory learning spaces with students.

name: Libertas School (Libertas)
description: The mission of Libertas is to be a school for human flourishing: cultivating minds, hands, and hearts of children in Memphis for lives of wonder, work, and love. Libertas opened in 2015 to turn around Tennessee’s second lowest achieving neighborhood elementary school; for the last several years, Libertas has ranked among the highest growth and achievement among schools serving at-risk students across Memphis. Libertas is Tennessee’s first charter school to implement Dr. Maria Montessori’s culturally rich, academically rigorous, developmentally responsive learning method. Libertas also operates the nation’s first dual-certification public Montessori teaching licensing program residing in urban schools.

name: Libertas School of Memphis
description: Libertas School of Memphis is dedicated to human flourishing, nurturing the minds, hands, and hearts of children for lives of wonder, work, and love. Since 2015, Libertas has transformed one of Tennessee’s lowest-performing schools into one of the highest-growing and achieving schools serving at-risk students. It is Tennessee’s first charter school to implement Dr. Maria Montessori’s culturally rich, academically rigorous methods, and operates the nation’s first dual-certification public Montessori teaching licensing program in urban schools.

name: Liberty STEAM Charter School
description: Liberty STEAM Charter School is a public charter school network that will launch in Sumter, SC in Fall 2021.The school intends to serve 1900 scholars at full enrollment across grades K-12 across four academies. Our STEAM focused model allows for hands on learning and critical thinking to develop for college and career success.

name: Lit
description: Lit, a program of Array Education, partners with education leaders to ensure every child becomes a reader. Its first digital platform, Bloom, delivers targeted foundational skills instruction, individualized practice, and real-time phoneme-specific corrective feedback.

name: Literacy Academy Collective
description: Literacy Academy Collective (LAC) is a public school that will launch in New York, New York in Fall 2023. The school intends to serve 400 students at full enrollment across grades K-8. Literacy Academy Collective designs and supports NYC Public Schools to bring evidence-based literacy instruction into the classroom. They partner with schools, families, community based organizations, and institutes of higher education to bring culturally relevant, structured literacy to the most marginalized students at the intersection of income, race and disability. Their aim is to alter life outcomes by allowing children to access learning through literacy.

name: LitLab.ai
description: Litlab.ai improves reading engagement and reading proficiency by giving K-2 teachers and students the power to generate personalized & decodable content using AI. Perfect for targeted reading practice, teachers can select specific skills for students to work on, and kids can co-author stories that are just-right for their skill levels and unique for their individual interests.

name: LivedX Inc
description: LivedX, an proprietary Artificial Intelligence powered platform enabling student success by converting their lived and learned experiences into transferable skills credentials including college credits.

name: Local Civics
description: Local Civics aims to transform civics education for young people across the United States. At its core, the model teaches young people about power: how to identify it, how to navigate it, and ultimately, how to harness their unique gifts to empower themselves and their communities. The model has standards-aligned, project-based curricular programming, and goal-based learning that is accessible to all students.

name: LUCA AI, LLC
description: LUCA’s beta reading platform supports struggling readers and students with dyslexia. Its patented StoryGen technology, proprietary speech recognition, and industry-leading word database create an adaptive experience that acts like a reading specialist for each student — saving teachers time and scaling support.

name: Magnolia Project
description: Magnolia Project is a 501(c)3 non-profit organization that builds pathways for experiential learning in California’s middle and high schools. We offer relevant Career Technical Education (CTE) centering student leadership and agency through Externship, Internship and Learning Journeys Pathways. Our students are the dreamers, doers, and decision-makers in their CTE experience. Through exploratory externships, immersive work-based internships, and curiosity filled Learning Journeys, Magnolia Project students will begin to define their Meaningful Next Phase, and develop the skills they need to achieve their goals.

name: Magpie Literacy
description: Effective reading technology depends on accurate voice recognition. Magpie Literacy’s goal is to build open-source automatic speech recognition software optimized for classrooms — capable of recognizing phoneme-level pronunciation, supporting diverse dialects and speech patterns, and providing feedback on literacy subskills.

name: Man Up Teacher Fellowship
description: Man Up Teacher Fellowship is a non-profit organization with the mission to give students in high poverty, urban and rural communities, particularly male students of color, access to high quality male teachers of color and to advance policies that promote equity in K – 12 schools. Man Up will provide fellows with opportunities to obtain licensure, earn a Master’s of Education, and become certified in Educational Leadership by creating a state approved teacher education preparation program that will provide tailored teacher education and targeted wraparound supports to Black male educators.

name: Margaret Hill Academy
description: Margaret Hill Academy in San Bernardino, California, seeks to revolutionize education for 6th-12th grade students by creating a dynamic, student-centered learning environment for at-promise youth. The school’s mission is built on the belief that true transformation happens through high-quality education, grounded in community school supports, challenge-based learning, and early college and career access.

name: MathTalk
description: MathTalk makes math fun, meaningful, and engaging for kids and families by connecting learning to local ecosystems. MathTalk products guide children through a multi-year community math journey, making math a valuable part of their everyday lives.

name: MCEL (Men of Color in Ed. Leadership)
description: Men of Color in Education Leadership (MCEL), an organization of leaders from across the educational spectrum, is hyper-focused on eliminating achievement and opportunity gaps for all students. Based on research and lived experiences, MCEL strongly supports highly effective leaders of color who are uniquely positioned to disrupt current ecosystems, lead equity throughout education, and serve as critical levers to student success.

name: MedReimagined
description: MedReimagined produces biomedical science kits, camps, and curricula all supported by an augmented education process that pairs practicing Black medical professionals with highly qualified classroom instructors to put worthy students from the communities with the greatest need on the path to becoming medical professionals. The MedReimagined concept was created by Dr. Damien Myers when he left his practice to pioneer a biomedical sciences program at an all-girls high school in Baltimore, MD, eventually earning recognition in the form of a presidential medal by then President Barak Obama. See more at www.medreimagined.com

name: Michigan Educator Workforce Initiative
description: Michigan Educator Workforce Initiative (MEWI) ensures Michigan’s schools and students have the diverse, high-quality teachers and school leaders they deserve. MEWI partners with Michigan’s K-12 districts and higher education institutions to design and support innovative approaches to recruiting and developing diverse teachers and school leaders. In 2023, MEWI launched the Talent Together Program, a statewide teacher preparation and certification pathway focused on growing and diversifying the Michigan educator workforce. MEWI also capture insights and learnings to share with systems leaders, policymakers, parents, and community leaders and convenes educators to share best practices, collaborate, and problem-solve.

name: Middle School of Innovative Learning (District 15 – NYCDOE)
description: Provide a flexible and inclusive environment that supports neurodiversity and cultural responsiveness. Students engage in project-based, real-world exploration, fostering critical thinking and creativity while connecting with their community

name: MindWorks Collaborative
description: CoPilot by MindWorks Collaborative is a game-changing partner for schools, meeting their workforce needs and creating conditions for diverse Special Education professionals to thrive. Our skilled CoPilot crew members not only provide direct compliance and flexible staffing support, but also model an affirming and asset-based approach to supporting neurodivergent students and their families. We’re here to make a lasting impact in the world of special education.

name: Mote Technologies, Inc
description: Conker (www.conker.ai) is an educational assessment platform that aims to create a more equitable learning experience by harnessing AI to remove the stress from assessment for teachers, by empowering students with learning differences and by creating assessments that reflect the background and experiences of students today. Conker has been used by more than 31,000 educators since it launched in January, and is brought to you by Mote Technologies, the team behind the Mote voice messaging app (www.mote.com).

name: MOXIE Public Schools
description: MOXIE Public Schools partners with Maine communities to create and sustain innovative, learner-centered schools where every K–12 student develops the skills, connections, and readiness to thrive together in a complex world.

name: MS 487 (NYC Department of Education)
description: To enable every young person to claim their power, evolve themselves, and change the world around them

name: NACA Inspired Schools Network
description: NISN is building a movement of students, families, and educators to create excellent schools relevant to the communities they serve. NISN works with fellows who are committed to Indigenous communities to establish schools in New Mexico and throughout the country that will create strong leaders who are academically prepared, secure in their identities, healthy, and ultimately transforming their communities. The current NISN network is working with communities in New Mexico, Colorado, California, South Dakota, and Minnesota. The Growing Together Fellowship will bring together Indigenous school leaders to stimulate a communal reimagining of what visionary leadership and a holistic, culturally integrated education might look like for all historically marginalized students.

name: Namahana Education Foundation
description: Namahana School is a tuition-free public charter middle and high school for the North Shore communities of Kaua‘i. Opening in 2025, the school will start with 7th and 8th grades and add a new grade each year until full capacity in 2030. Guided by the Big Picture Learning network and ‘āina-based learning, Namahana’s model emphasizes connections with students’ communities and natural environments to build critical skills for solving real-world challenges.

name: NarrateAR LLC
description: Like our own child, one in six students struggle with special needs. We build personalized AI co-pilots for learning that grow with your child to unlock their full potential.

name: National Accelerator of Autism Charter Schools
description: The National Accelerator of Autism Charter Schools (NAACS) supports founding teams dedicated to starting autism-focused charter schools by offering fellowships, training, resources, and a national support network. NAACS envisions accelerating the opening of an autism charter school in every major city in America.

name: Navigate the Maze to Achievement, Inc.
description: Navigate the Maze to Achievement (NTMA) aims to increase the number of Black and Latinx students enrolled in specialized and selective high schools in New York City (NYC), and support their success from enrollment to graduation and beyond. In NYC, Black and Latinx students make up 70% of all public high school students but less than 9% of students in specialized high schools. To combat this disparity, NTMA provides the information, strategies, and preparation needed to compete and succeed in high-performing schools.

name: New Classrooms
description: New Classrooms designs innovative instructional models and digital learning tools that transform student achievement by reimagining how time, talent, technology, and space are used to help teachers personalize learning.

name: New Village Academy
description: New Village Academy is tuition-free, public charter high school where each student crafts their own pathway to success in career, character, and civic engagement with the city of Annapolis as their classroom. New Village Academy ensures every student is KNOWN as a unique individual, CONNECTED to community-based learning and networks, EMPOWERED with agency over their personal growth, and ACCOUNTABLE to meaningful learning in the real world, so that they have the WILL and the SKILL to take charge of their education and future.

name: Newark Opportunity Youth Network
description: Newark Opportunity Youth Network (NOYN) launched in 2016 with a multi-pronged approach to improving outcomes for opportunity youth in their community and across New Jersey. To execute this mission, their network is comprised of four key elements: Education, Workforce Development, Policy Advocacy, and Systems Building. Every city and region is unique, but when this approach is localized to a particular community’s needs, only then, can large-scale, sustainable impact for opportunity youth become possible. NOYN looks to address the underrepresentation of male educators of color and the broader teacher shortage by increasing high school seniors’ and pre-service teachers’ interest in teaching and supporting male educators of color through their My Brother’s Keeper Newark initiative.

name: North Edgecombe High School of Innovation (Edgecombe County Public Schools)
description: 2648 International Blvd Ste 301, Oakland, CA 94601  |    EIN: 94-3281780   |info@newschools.org|Accessibility Statement

name: Novel Education Partners
description: Novel partners with K-12 schools to achieve their vision for equitable and rigorous ELA instruction. By collaborating with schools, Novel unlocks the potential of high-quality, culturally responsive curricula, supporting the growth of students, teachers, and leaders to build lifelong learners.

name: Oakland Natives Give Back Fund, Inc.
description: Oakland Natives Give Back Fund Inc. (ONGB) is an independent 501(c)(3) non-profit organization dedicated to serving communities across the nation through efforts to reduce educational disparities. Our mission is to create and scale innovative solutions that empower young people and improve educational equity.

name: OJI:SDA’ Sustainable Indigenous Futures
description: OJI:SDA’ aims to implement a land-based academic curriculum by partnering with K-12 teachers and community elders. Land-based learning uses an Indigenous and environmentally-focused approach by recognizing the deep connection and relationship to land. Through this curriculum, students will engage in lessons about humanities, mathematics and sciences, while also learning about indigenous cultures and values and how to care for the Earth.

name: Oko Labs, Inc.
description: OKO Labs is dedicated to transforming education by providing high-quality, collaborative learning experiences that are accessible and engaging for all students. Using AI-powered small-group instruction, OKO Labs empowers educators to personalize learning and foster deeper understanding, creating a more equitable and effective environment where every student thrives.

name: One in Five
description: OneInFive seeks to empower Indiana youth with lived mental health experience with exposure to and practice with careers in mental health, to become peer mental health recovery specialists, and improve outcomes for the 20% of youth most impacted by the lack of affordable mental health care.

name: Open Source Phonics
description: The Open Source Phonics website was launched in 2021 and published the 120 lessons and decodable story groups. Open Source Phonics, Inc. was incorporated in Maryland in January of 2022.

name: OpenLiteracy
description: OpenLiteracy’s AI Pathways curriculum maximizes the impact of 1:1 and small-group reading interventions for K-8 students. Its AI-driven tool enables educators to create personalized learning pathways, delivering tailored literacy lessons that meet each student’s needs.

name: OpenSeat
description: OpenSeat adds much-needed capacity for K-12 schools, and in partnership with schools, serves as an extension of a school’s social-emotional and counseling supports. OpenSeat provides students 1-on-1 virtual access to a diverse team of trained and supervised mental wellness professionals – including on nights, weekends, and breaks when students often need additional support.

name: OurWorlds
description: OurWorlds innovates emerging technologies to preserve and advance history, culture, current events, arts, music and language. OurWorlds is an immersive reality communication platform that uses geolocation to transform public and private spaces for education and entertainment.

name: Outline It, Inc.
description: Outline is a writing platform that breaks down the writing process into smaller parts, by supporting students through the brainstorming and organizing parts of essay writing. Many students have a difficult time writing essays and research has shown that when digital tools are used to teach writing, students are highly motivated to write. Created with teacher input, Outline is designed to help students better organize their thoughts, form a cohesive narrative out of multiple stories, distill ideas into one-sentence summaries, and learn the fundamentals of writing a strong outline. Using the Outline platform, students are able to generate essay ideas and organize these ideas to begin creating a sequence for their essay. Outline helps students summarize their ideas into main points, prompts them with transition words to link their ideas together and encourages them to fill in their ideas with additional sentences. Students then have an outline of their essay that they’re able to download as a foundation for additional writing. The approach is based on a proprietary essay brainstorming and structuring method developed from over 10 years of working with teenagers one-on-one, with 95% of students getting into one of their top 3 colleges and receiving more than $10 million in scholarships.

name: Pahara Institute
description: Pahara Institute’s mission is to strengthen and sustain values-driven leaders who are transforming public education so that every child is prepared to lead a free and fulfilling life.

name: Palm Beach School for Autism
description: The mission of Palm Beach School for Autism is to provide innovative education and behavioral supports that empower individuals with autism.

name: Paloma Learning Inc.
description: Paloma is an edtech company that harnesses generative AI to unleash parents’ untapped teaching potential. Our product is a platform and marketplace that connects teachers to families and families to learning resources. Paloma will make it so easy and gratifying for teachers to engage families in student learning that doing so becomes standard practice.

name: Parent Power Lab
description: The Parent Power Lab exists to amplify the collective agency of Parents to impact public education in transformative ways by eliminating the institutional and systemic silencing of the collective parent voice in urban education through the Parent Organizing Institute.

name: Pathlitics
description: Pathlitics empowers students with learning differences to confidently plan their paths after high school. Its online hub centralizes a robust dataset of programs, colleges, and jobs, while offering meaningful insights from students with similar lived experiences. Pathlitics also serves as a powerful transition planning tool for high school counselors, helping them support neurodivergent students while ensuring compliance with federal special education law.

name: Pathway Academy
description: Pathway Academy is an innovative educational model serving vulnerable populations. The approach is founded on best practices in mastery learning, literacy, character building, and relevant real-world learning experiences.

name: Pathways to Accelerate a Career Experience (Casa Grande Union High School District)
description: NewSchools funding will assist in the development of a new Pathways to Accelerate a Career Experience (PACE) school. PACE will engage in personalized learning to meet each students individualized needs. Students will have access to college preparation, dual credit, internships and/or employment based on their individual goals. Graduates would carry with them marketable and college ready skill sets.

name: Pathways to Accelerate a Career Experience (Casa Grande Union High School District)
description: NewSchools funding will assist in the development of a new Pathways to Accelerate a Career Experience (PACE) school. PACE will engage in personalized learning to meet each students individualized needs. Students will have access to college preparation, dual credit, internships and/or employment based on their individual goals. Graduates would carry with them marketable and college ready skill sets.

name: PeerTeach Inc
description: PeerTeach is a data-driven peer tutoring platform for 3rd-8th grade math, developed through research at Stanford University. It evaluates students’ mastery of math concepts, predicts the most effective tutoring pairs, and engages them in gamified peer coaching. PeerTeach empowers students to build leadership, confidence, and a love of math while supporting their peers.

name: Philadelphia Middle College Foundation
description: All youth deserve quality schools designed to educate and empower them with greater access to college completion, career success, and secure lives. Philadelphia Middle College Foundation (PMCF) launched in 2023 to create an early/middle college charter school offering innovative secondary education that inspires youth to thrive. The school will provide step-by-step pathways to earn an associate’s degree or industry certifications alongside a high school diploma, focusing on high-demand industries like healthcare and technology. PMCF’s core philosophy is equity through education, ensuring a jump-start into postsecondary life.

name: Playground IEP
description: An AI-powered tool that combines a comprehensive directory of postsecondary options—jobs, colleges, and trades—with a personalized transition inventory that matches students with disabilities to opportunities based on their strengths and interests.

name: Profound Ladies
description: Profound Ladies’ vision is to increase the recruitment and retention of Black and Indigenous women in education. Profound Ladies equips Black and Indigenous women with the mentorship, training, leadership, and career development needed for these teachers to interrupt inequitable outcomes for their students. Profound Ladies envisions that every student of Black and Indigenous female teachers will realize the impact of learning from a thriving woman of color.

name: Project OneTen
description: Project OneTen seeks to address the achievement gap between Black and Latino/x boys and their peers. Project OneTen’s mission is to engage and mentor Black and Latino/x boys who attend Chicago Public Schools during their transition from eighth grade to ninth grade, teaching them to recognize and develop their strengths, academic confidence, and innate brilliance.

name: Project Read AI
description: Project Read is an AI co-teacher designed to support the Science of Reading. It provides teachers and students with evidence-based, structured literacy tools to accelerate learning and improve reading outcomes.

name: Propel America
description: Propel America, founded in 2019 to build supported alternative pathways from high school into a meaningful career in healthcare, is eager to launch a new initiative to generate awareness of and readiness for a jobs-first higher education pathway amongst 9-12th graders. Propel will launch in 2024 an updated K-12 strategy that involves working with young people directly and expose more adults working in secondary schools to jobs-first higher education so more high school students can build job readiness and career planning skills.

name: Public Impact
description: Public Impact’s mission is to improve education dramatically for all students, especially low-income students, students of color, and other students whose needs historically have not been well met. Public Impact’s work is designed to contribute to dramatic improvements in the quality of education and related policy supports in the United States. The Opportunity Culture initiative, a program founded by the Public Impact team, extends the reach of excellent teachers and their teams to more students, for more pay, within regular budgets. This initiative works to find, develop, retain, and expand the impact of great teachers and leaders vital to operating great schools.

name: Queens International 6-12 School
