description: Internationals Network for Public Schools addresses educational justice by expanding opportunities for adolescent English learners who have recently arrived in U.S. schools and are systematically excluded and underserved in public education. While focused on this population, Internationals Network seeks to influence broader research, policy, and practice to advance innovation and equitable opportunities in education.

name: Quill.org
description: Quill.org’s mission is to enable 10 million 4th-12th grade students attending low-income, Title-1 schools to become strong readers, writers, and critical thinkers in the next five years. Quill currently serves 12,000 schools across the United States (8,000 Title-1 schools) through free, AI-powered literacy tools that provide students with real-time, actionable feedback and coaching so that students can revise their work and quickly build their literacy skills. While Quill primarily serves English Language Arts classrooms today, support from NewSchools Venture Fund is enabling Quill to provide “writing across the curriculum” by developing new AI-powered curricula for Social Studies and STEM classrooms.

name: Radical Arts Academy Denver (Denver Public Schools)
description: Radical Arts Academy of Denver (RAAD) is an innovative ECE-8th grade school/program with a unique Arts Based Learning (ABL) model, approved to open in the Far Northeast area of the Denver Public Schools District in Fall of 2024.

name: Reconstruction US, Inc
description: Reconstruction is the #1 provider of holistic, supplemental, culturally relevant curriculum, centered on the Black experience. Our comprehensive solutions create transformative learning experiences that inspire student empowerment and equip teachers to effectively educate on culturally responsive content. Young minds thrive when they see themselves and their communities reflected in what they’re learning. And all students stand to gain from learning about the full scope of Black contributions to our world.

name: Relay Graduate School of Education
description: Relay Graduate School of Education (Relay) is a national, accredited nonprofit institution of higher education dedicated to teacher and school leadership preparation and development. Relay’s mission is to ensure that all students are taught by outstanding educators, and envisions a more just world where every student has access to excellent teachers and a clear path to a fulfilling life.

name: Responsive Arts and STEAM Academy (Denver Public Schools)
description: 2648 International Blvd Ste 301, Oakland, CA 94601  |    EIN: 94-3281780   |info@newschools.org|Accessibility Statement

name: RISE Child Development Center
description: RISE introduces a play-based, anti-racist and liberatory curriculum for early childhood education. Their educational model includes curricula and professional development modules / training. While they have built out an early version of this solution, the RISE team will continue to iterate on the curriculum by leveraging existing research-based practices that are aligned to preschool standards. The teacher professional development component supports educators and administrators to create an anti-racist and liberatory environment for children. This 16-week training includes a behavioral component (e.g., how to handle different student behavior, how to structure a class team) as well as lesson plans.

name: Rochester Education Association for the Development of Youth, Inc. (READY)
description: READY emerged from a two-year community engagement process led by Dr. Shanai Lee and supported by a number of community-based organizations, national and local foundations, and Rochester community members. READY’s Parent Action Academy builds parents’ and caregivers’ of color capacities and abilities to advocate for the educational change needed for their children to access and attend quality K-12 schools. READY also partners with Rochester families, educators, community members, and existing not-for-profits to launch and sustain high-quality schools for the children who need them most.

name: Rock Academy
description: The mission of The Rock Academy is to re-engage disconnected youth and ensure they succeed in the postsecondary pathway of their choice. We envision a world where all young people have the power to shape their own futures.

name: Rosebud Sioux Tribe Education Department
description: The Rosebud Sioux Tribe Education Department seeks to form welcoming and trusting relationships between school system leaders and families by ensuring families have the confidence, knowledge and skills, and opportunity to be equal partners in their Wakanyeja’s (children’s) educational experiences through the Tiospaye Together Council.

name: Rural Opportunity Institute (ROI)
description: Rural Opportunity Institute (ROI) is a community-based social venture studio that builds the capacity of rural communities to support people’s healing from generational trauma to achieve health, safety, connection, and self-determination. ROI’s newest initiative “10x Mental Health Supports for Students” helps schools and community-based organizations to put the systems and structures in place to leverage Medicaid funding so they can increase student access to mental health services.

name: Rural Opportunity Institute (ROI)
description: Rural Opportunity Institute (ROI) is a community-based social venture studio that builds the capacity of rural communities to support people’s healing from generational trauma to achieve health, safety, connection, and self-determination. ROI’s newest initiative “10x Mental Health Supports for Students” helps schools and community-based organizations to put the systems and structures in place to leverage Medicaid funding so they can increase student access to mental health services.

name: Sankofa Montessori
description: Sankofa Montessori is a public school that will launch in Atlanta, Georgia in Fall 2023. The school intends to serve 550 students at full enrollment across grades K-6. Through a culturally responsive, child and family-centered Montessori education, Sankofa Montessori ensures that all children develop the knowledge, skills, and agency to lead purposeful lives. The vision of Sankofa Montessori is to harness each child’s inner drive to learn by creating a vibrant community of learners where each child is respected, has their identity affirmed, and has their curiosity encouraged.

name: Sea Islands Heritage Academy
description: Sea Islands Heritage Academy is a community co-designed, 6-12th grade, place-based public charter school opening in July 2024. Rooted in Gullah Geechee practices and beliefs, Sea Islands Heritage Academy prioritizes personalized pathways in a communal learning environment.

name: SELF
description: SELF’s vision is an educational system where all students are celebrated, treated with dignity, and their strengths are leveraged to empower authentic and individualized learning. Our mission is to empower schools to create welcoming and effective communities that enable all students to achieve their fullest potential. The National Leader Fellowship, established in 2022, will bring together school leaders and special education coordinators in an intensive two-year fellowship consisting of specialized training, coaching, professional learning communities, peer reviews and visits to exemplary special education programs. This cohort of national leaders will collaborate and return to their respective schools and districts with rich and diverse plans of action.

name: Ser Familia, Inc.
description: Ser Familia is a non-profit organization empowering Latino families through educational programs, workshops, and retreats that allow them to thrive and enjoy a healthy family environment. Their holistic approach brings a sense of wholeness to people’s lives that allows them to improve their “now” and face “what’s next” with a positive outlook. Ser Familia recently launched an academic & overall well-being youth programming meets the growing mental health & educational needs by 1. addressing the widening educational & health/wellbeing gaps, 2. lack of digital/technology access, & 3. learning loss from COVID. All services, including tutoring, mentoring, physical activities, hands-on learning, life skills & career exploration, health/wellbeing sessions, & counseling are based on culturally/linguistically proficient model programming & proven-effective practices.

name: SF Parent Coalition
description: San Francisco (SF) Parent Coalition centers the needs of children and youth in San Francisco public schools by bringing together a diverse network of parents and caregivers to advocate for a thriving, equitable school system. SF Parent Coalition envisions a future where San Francisco is one of the best places in the country for children and youth of all races, socioeconomic backgrounds, and abilities to access and obtain an excellent public education.

name: Sims-Fayola Foundation
description: The Sims-Fayola Foundation was launched in 2014 as a 501(c)(3) to help change this narrative. We boldly accept the challenge to improve the lives of young men and boys of color by addressing the obstacles (educational and societal) that directly affect their life outcomes. We use original programming and awareness campaigns, partner with established community foundations and organizations, and individual schools and districts to address these stubbornly persistent issues for urban males. Our programs, services, mission, and values are driven by three pillars: 1. Building Awareness, 2. Building Equity by Design 3. Building Capacity. Our desired outcome is to provide direction, education, guidance, resources, direct support, and ultimately relief in these areas so the futures of these young men look like their dreams. The Sims-Fayola Foundation seeks to achieve their mission by launching a new fellowship designed to equip male teachers of color with equity-centered and gender-specific tools, resources, and support systems needed to increase their pedagogical knowledge and confidence.

name: Sledge Institute
description: The Sledge Institute is a K-12 charter school in rural northeastern North Carolina’s Kerr Tar region. It connects curriculum to students’ lived experiences, offering opportunities to learn from industry professionals and reigniting exploration and wonder in education. With a learner-centered model based on six key elements, students explore essential questions like, “Why do I need to learn this?”, “When will I use this?”, and “What if…?”

name: Snorkl
description: Snorkl provides students with immediate, personalized feedback on their thinking and problem solving. Using its multimodal tools, students record visual and verbal explanations to show their reasoning and receive real-time support tailored to their work.

name: Social Cipher
description: Social Cipher builds social-emotional learning video games that help youth of all neurotypes understand themselves and those around them, and software that helps educators and counselors better understand their students’ social emotional well being.

name: Social Optics
description: Social Optics is a web based social communication solution for neurodivergent teens. We believe that understanding yourself and the science of communication is the foundation for positive self-esteem, relationships, and resilience. Social Optics allows schools to combine direct instruction, self-reflection and independent learning activities to help students develop authentic and empathetic communication skills.

name: South Shore Charter School
description: South Shore Charter School’s mission is to cultivate in our students the tenacity, integrity, and curiosity needed to become innovative and socially responsible leaders, ready to face and solve the ever-changing challenges facing our society.

name: Southern Nevada Black Educators Initiative
description: The Southern Nevada Black Educators Initiative (SNBEI) addresses ongoing teacher recruitment and retention issues in Nevada by exposing the community to the uplifting impact of teachers of color and investing in human capital. SNBEI provides a continuum of support and advocacy to Black preservice and in-service educators through professional development services, critical mentorship, intentional recruitment and retention strategies and legislative advocacy in their Amplifying Black Voices Community Outreach initiative.

name: Sown To Grow, Inc
description: Sown to Grow was part of the Expanded Definition of Student Success Ed Tech Challenge. Funds supported Sown to Grow’s student-led goal setting, reflection, and metacognition platform that builds better, more confident learners.

name: Spelligent Education
description: Spelligent seeks to ensure communities, caretakers, educators, and students understand how learners vary and understand how to attend to this variability in school and beyond. We do this by helping schools design school-wide systems that center learner variability, partnering with education support organizations to integrate the lens of learner variability into their programs, elevating voices through storytelling across multiple media platforms

name: Springpoint
description: Transformative Learning Experiences (TLEs) are project-based curricula that make high school meaningful. Developed by Springpoint, TLEs are deeply researched, youth-centered, informed by culturally responsive pedagogy, and richly supported for teacher implementation. Explicitly designed with both rigor and purpose in mind, TLEs focus on real-world problems that leverage students’ passions and speak to their interests and identities. They require students to engage in deep thinking and practice of essential post-secondary skills, create an authentic product, engage with experts and members of the community, and present their work to an authentic audience.

name: St. Louis Voices Academy of Media Arts
description: St. Louis Voices Academy of Media Arts is a free, public charter school that leverages media arts and storytelling to equip students with the agency to excel academically, author their own futures, and make meaningful contributions in their communities. SLVA is launching with kindergarten and first grade this fall and will grow a grade each year until fifth grade.

name: Start Lighthouse
description: Start Lighthouse, a 501(c)3 nonprofit founded in the Bronx, NY, is dedicated to advancing childhood literacy through a social justice lens. They achieve this through three key initiatives: Literacy Hubs, where abandoned library spaces are revitalized to serve as permanent homes for literacy programs; Literary Adventures, featuring interactive workshops led by BIPOC authors and illustrators to foster students’ love for reading and writing; and Home Libraries, providing students with diverse, free, and inclusive books each month to build their home libraries, ensuring representation in their reading materials.

name: STEM4Real
description: ​STEM4Real is committed to providing quality STEM professional learning infused with principles of equity, anti-bias and justice-centered pedagogy so that every student has access to a rigorous and joyful STEM education. We provide collaborative professional learning for educators committed to equity and anti-racism. We combine standards-based content + anti-racist pedagogy. Standards + Justice, you don’t have to choose. We use Lesson Study to CONNECT with our students, CREATE culturally responsive instruction and CULTIVATE a culture of anti-bias, anti-racism and belonging.

name: Step Up Tutoring
description: Step Up delivers virtual, high-impact 1:1 tutoring to underserved students in grades 2–6. It is developing two GenAI tools to help tutors improve instruction: a post-session feedback tool that reinforces effective practices and a real-time assistant supporting math explanations and relationship-building.

name: Stepmojo Education, LLC
description: Stepmojo supports students to take steps to increase their mojo (special talents). Stepmojo believes that the high school of the future embraces a blended learning approach – leveraging the best of a strong, in-person high school community with the best of online instruction, allowing us to radically expand the set of high-quality course options available to our young people. To facilitate this vision, they offer a diverse array of high-quality, live online courses from best-in-class partners through one easy-to-use platform. In their hybrid delivery model, classes are taught by engaging, expert online teachers from the course partners while an on-site learning coach or proctor at the school is circulating and supporting the learning in a classroom or communal space (e.g., library, study hall, etc.). Stepmojo’s goal is to provide students everywhere with more equitable access to exceptional content and teachers – giving them more agency to pursue personal interests, further academic goals, and explore potential college and career paths.

name: Strived.io
description: Strived.io is an AI-powered data tool for district leaders, school leaders, and teachers that collaborates with top Edtech vendors, assessment providers, and classroom data to generate actionable insights. By mapping student learning through integrated skills and standards, Strived.io helps educators coach each student effectively. It will also include behavior and emotional factors to provide a comprehensive understanding of how each child learns best.

name: Substantial Classrooms
description: Substantial was founded to unlock the potential of substitute teaching, one of the most overlooked, under-designed areas of public education. We developed SubSchool, our proven professional development platform, to provide substitute teachers with high-quality, tailored training and support. With SubSchool, we aim to cultivate the skills and confidence that make subs more effective in the classroom, and more likely to continue working in education.

name: Surge Institute
description: The Surge Institute’s mission is to educate and develop leaders of color who create transformative change for young people, their families and our broader communities.

name: T.E.E.C.H. (Teaching for Equity, Empowerment and Community Healing)
description: TEECH stands for Teaching for Equity, Empowerment and Community Healing. Unlike most existing teacher-training programs that do not prepare culturally responsive or justice-oriented teachers, TEECH aims to address the disconnect between school and students’ lives and their communities by training teachers as community workers and advocates, and by increasing their capacity to effectively serve the communities in which they teach. By drawing on their lived experiences and connections to community, TEECHers will be able to center their curriculum, practices, and relationships around culturally responsive practices—including the trajectory of leadership, policies and priorities of schools.

name: Tacoma Public Schools
description: Maritime 253 connects students from the south sound with maritime and skilled trades careers through hands-on learning, industry partnerships, and certification pathways, preparing a future-ready workforce for our region.

name: Talkido
description: Talkido is a screen-free language support system designed to empower young learners with diverse needs in PreK to K5. Through a personalized approach, Talkido helps children build essential listening, attention, speaking, and literacy skills. Its accessible, interactive tools foster social and cognitive development, supporting children in reaching their full potential.

name: Tampa Early College Academy
description: Tampa Early College Academy (TECA) transforms learning for students in grades 6–12 through a rigorous early college model that blends middle and high school with college. TECA equips students with the skills, support, and academic foundation needed to succeed in college and career.

name: Tapestry Public Charter School (Tapestry)
description: Tapestry Public Charter School was founded with a focus on creating a new educational model that supports the unique needs of students on the autism spectrum. Tapestry’s mission is to offer an inclusive, individualized learning environment for grades 6-12 that is academically and socially engaging for both neurotypical students and those with autism. Tapestry seeks to replicate its co-teaching model and open an additional campus in the Atlanta Public School district to support even more students in the metro Atlanta community.

name: TasselTurn
description: TasselTurn (TT) is an edtech nonprofit creating pathways to graduation and college for housing-insecure youth and youth in foster care. TasellTurn aims to shift the narrative for foster-involved youth to one where all students have access to opportunities and information that will allow them to reach their post-secondary educational aspirations.

name: Teach Indy, Inc.
description: Teach Indy’s mission is to recruit, convene, develop, and recognize educators of color in Indianapolis by cultivating a teacher-centered community, leading a county-wide effort to reimagine the role of a teacher, and convening educators as thought leaders.  They seek to create an inclusive, diverse, sustainable education system that retains its high-quality educators of color and ensures that all Indianapolis students thrive.

name: Teachers Like Me
description: Teachers Like Me (TLM) aims to increase the number of Black teachers by removing barriers to entry and providing the support needed to be effective and responsive to the academic and social needs of all students, specifically students of color. TLM recruits, develops, and retains both aspiring and early career Black educators. TLM supports prospective teachers from successful college graduation to teacher certification and into their early-career. They also provide affordable housing and targeted training to ensure teachers are prepared to not only enter, but succeed and sustain within the classroom.

name: Teachley LLC
description: Teachley is developing Tutoring with Teachley Math, a research-based, high-dosage small-group tutoring program that improves K–5 math outcomes. The program blends authentic games, multimedia resources, and problem-solving within a coherent scope and sequence.

name: TeachStart
description: TeachStart was founded on the core belief that, while there may be a teacher shortage, there are still prospective educators who want to teach. Many of these prospective educators identify as people of color. TeachStart provides a pathway for prospective teachers of color to enter the classroom by removing the barriers they commonly face.  TeachStart educators serve as substitute teachers while earning a debt-free credential, solving both schools’ immediate need for substitutes and their long-term need for more diverse teachers.

name: Texas A&M-San Antonio Institute for School and Community Partnerships
description: A&M San Antonio’s Institute for School and Community Partnerships collaborates with Texas A&M University at San Antonio and local partners to support students through innovative practices, research, and equitable policy. Its mission is to help students reach their full potential by transforming school systems, improving early intervention services, and fostering PK-12 district partnerships that prepare students for college and high-demand careers. The Institute serves as a hub for excellence in child and youth development, career-ready models, and workforce opportunities.

name: The Ability Challenge
description: The Ability Challenge (ABC) partners with schools and districts to build inclusive communities and world-class special education programs. ABC provides school leaders and educators with tailored training, support, and tools to transform services for students with disabilities, ensuring access, belonging, and success for the 1 in 5 students with learning differences. Using the research-backed THRIVE Framework, ABC offers customized roadmaps, leadership support, educator training, and targeted solutions to address special education challenges.

name: The Academy of Okmulgee
description: The Academy of Seminole is a PreK to 12th grade free, public life planning school. We are based on the principals of inclusion and empowerment. So far our results have been phenomenal. The next school, The Academy of Okmulgee, will open in 2024.

name: The AI Education Project (aiEDU)
description: The AI Education Project (aiEDU) is a 501(c)(3) non-profit centering equity and accessibility in AI education. We educate students—especially those disproportionately impacted by AI and automation—with the conceptual knowledge and skills they need to thrive as future workers, creators, consumers, and citizens. We’re dedicated to create equitable educational experiences to excite and empower learners everywhere with AI literacy.

name: The Anchor School
description: The Anchor School is a village of educators, families, and community members that partners with 6th through 12th grade students who are developing the knowledge, skills, and confidence they need to thrive in school and beyond. Through our commitment to excellent instruction, community partnerships, and holistic student development, we cultivate anchors that build a more just and equitable future.

name: The Apti Model
description: The APTI Model is developing a K-5 literacy curriculum and social-emotional learning (SEL) framework that provides students with diverse texts and personalized instruction. The model integrates the CASEL Framework to promote SEL skills like emotional health, positive self-identity and strong interpersonal skills alongside literacy development through a four-step approach: A-Analyze, P-Plan, T-Teach, and I-Inspire. The model also aims to increase students’ academic success through collaborative learning and culturally relevant lessons.

name: The ASSISTments Foundation
description: ASSISTments is a math practice and assessment platform proven to improve student achievement by infusing evidence-based strategies into teaching and learning. ASSISTments-Powered offers a system-wide solution for school districts, providing comprehensive support and real-time analytics to help districts implement Illustrative Math and Open Up Resources curricula effectively.

name: The Calculus Project
description: The Calculus Project aims to scale seven research-supported strategies that lead to higher level math success for Black, Latino, and low income students in 10 new district partners over the next two years. The model is a unique assemblage of research-supportive components defined by its comprehensiveness, high expectations, cultural sensitivity, and commitment to sustainability. We are compelled by the fact that Adrian and his team have demonstrated the ability to partner with districts and systematically improve math proficiency (e.g., through MCAS scores in Massachusetts).

name: The Canizales Group
description: “Transforming lives of children, families, and communities through quality, bilingual anti-racist professional development and capacity building for teachers, schools, and system leaders.” We envision anti-racist structures in districts, schools, and classrooms where emerging bilingual students and students of color are honored, loved, challenged, and successful in order to use their socio-cultural capacities to be anti-racist change agents in their own communities. Our mission is to support individuals and educational institutions committed to anti-racism and focused on redefining, reimagining, and challenging the way we serve emergent bilinguals and students of color. We provide the coaching and tools necessary to revolutionize and decolonize systems, structures, and programming that directly impact students and their families.

name: The Center for Urban Education and Advocacy
description: The mission of the Center for Urban Education and Advocacy (CUEA) is to provide a transformative impact on urban communities through education. One of the most pressing needs in urban communities is finding effective teachers of color to teach in urban schools. CUEA seeks to address this issue by inspiring high school students of color to become teachers, providing wrap-around support to aspiring teachers of color, and amplifying the voices of current teachers of color in order to drive change in urban schools, school systems, and teacher preparation programs.

name: The Craft Education System, Inc.
description: Craft Education Systems helps states end their teacher shortages by serving as the “Turbo Tax” for teacher apprenticeship reporting. It automates the collection of thousands of data points to save time of districts, educator preparation providers and apprentices. Craft uses this data to understand the gaps and shortage areas within districts and states to best target solutions to the teacher shortage.

name: The Dyscalculia Training & Research Institute
description: The DTRI supports individuals with dyscalculia, the math learning disability, through awareness programs, educator training, and direct math support for K–16 students. We also serve people with other forms of neurodivergence, including specific learning disorders, ADHD, and autism.

name: The Emancipated, LLC
description: EmancipatED seeks to address the limited, trauma-centered historical narratives of Black people throughout the Americas by expanding awareness of rebel Black communities, known as Maroon communities. This hidden Black history is not taught in K-12 classrooms, and the few comprehensive resources about Maroon communities are books written for collegiate or graduate students.

name: The Inspired Community Project
description: The Inspired Community Project is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing no-cost early intervention services to neurodivergent children, advocacy coaching for their families, and specialized career training and certification support for Bronx community members. Creating a self-sustaining, hyper-local, special education resources system, of the community for the community.

name: The Institute for Anti-Racist Education, Inc.
description: The Institute for Anti-Racist Education is developing the Create Freedom Learning Model, which includes a cross-disciplinary curriculum and instruction design tool that aims to enhance student engagement and learning. The model centers abolitionist SEL practices (which elevate healing, critical care, transformative dialogue, and models joy, vulnerability, and community) while aligning to state educational standards and mandates. The curriculum has quarterly units that span across different subject areas and each unit offers diverse topics where students explore various issues and create action plans to implement their solutions in small groups. In addition to the curriculum, the model includes professional learning communities for teachers and opportunities to collaborate with one another.

name: The Legacy School
description: The Legacy School is a public school that will launch in Cleveland, Ohio in Fall 2023. The school intends to serve 400 students at full enrollment across grades 9-12. The Legacy School aims to increase college preparedness in historically underrepresented communities through a culturally responsive early college academic experience that uses learning expeditions to deepen students understanding of self, academics, and how they will make their mark on the world. The Legacy School uses problem-and place-based learning to invoke curiosity, inspire student agency.

name: The Lexsee Reading Company
description: The Lexsee Reading Company helps middle and high school students build fluency, confidence, and comprehension through real-time, interactive support. Educators can track ORF assignments, monitor progress, and review smart, augmented session notes.

name: The Meliora School
description: The Meliora School is a proposed 6th–12th grade public charter school in Gwinnett County, co-designed with the community to offer mastery-based learning, entrepreneurship, and computer science. Rooted in equity and innovation, we continually pursue better outcomes for every scholar we serve.

name: The Museum School of East Dallas
description: The Museum School of East Dallas is a public charter school launching in Fall 2026. It integrates museum-based, immersive learning with rigorous academics. Designed through a community co-design process, it serves K–8 students with a focus on high-quality work and real-world exploration.

name: The National Fellowship of Black and Latino Male Educators (NFBLME)
description: NFBLME’s purpose is to create a dynamic, life-changing, empowering fellowship for Black and Latino males that springboards them into principal, senior level, and C-suite roles, increasing the number, longevity, and impact of Black and Latino males in the education sector. The Fellowship runs two concurrent national cohorts through a two year program, but also serves a growing number of localized cohorts throughout the country.

name: The Practice Space
description: The Practice Space elevates underrepresented voices through public speaking education. They offer inclusive and accessible educational content to build oral literacy skills, including curriculum and coaching to help educators support speaking and listening for English Learners. Their award-winning direct service programs help young people own the power of their voices through speech and debate and feel brave enough to speak as they navigate an inequitable world.

name: The Read Read
description: The Read Read™ is the first device that allows children to independently learn and practice phonics using best-practice literacy instruction. Children with various disabilities (including visual impairment and blindness, dyslexia, ASD, ADHD, and ODD) are using The Read Read™ to independently learn and practice phonics and braille. Developed at Harvard, with engineers from MIT, The Read Read™ is producing game-changing results, especially for kids with special needs: during our 5-month pilot, kids previously classified as ‘non-readers’ were able to learn to read using our device.

name: The Roots Initiative
description: Our mantra at The Roots Initiative is, “When you intentionally and authentically invest in Black youth, they will then have everything they need to be the next set of leaders to drive radical change.” Our programming, co-collaborated with our young leaders, is grounded in and promotes the principles of cultural identity restoration, social and emotional wellness, building collective responsibility, and most importantly, joy. Coupled with their genius, we believe these tenets equip them with the hope, vision, resistance and power to chart the course towards liberated communities.

name: The Simple Vue Academy
description: The Simple Vue Academy (SVA) provides a sustainable pathway to entrepreneurship for students in grades 6-12 through STREAM-based education, business and financial management guidance, and a supportive community. SVA’s entrepreneurial learning model empowers students to take ownership of their education and equips them with real-world skills to turn ideas into action. The interdisciplinary curriculum integrates science, technology, reading/writing, engineering, arts, and mathematics, connecting learning to real-life experiences through hands-on, authentic education.

name: The Teaching Well
description: The Teaching Well works in partnership with schools to more effectively support, retain, and leverage the brilliance of their educators. Together, we heal adult culture and create thriving school ecosystems by providing tools for healthy dialogue, emotional regulation, and mindful stress resilience. Their program, Retention at the Roots, seeks to design, prototype, and codify a replicable teacher preparation model that considers the whole person, roots in wellness, and supports educators to find a sustainable pathway to longevity. The organization partners with graduate schools and credentialing programs to re-imagine teacher preparation. Wellness will not be an add on, but foundational.

name: Thinkverse
description: Thinkverse is an AI-powered math tutor and teaching assistant for grades 3–12. It provides personalized scaffolding and real-time feedback in more than 100 languages, without revealing answers. Fully aligned with state standards, it integrates seamlessly with over 50 curricula and assessments.

name: Thrive
description: 2648 International Blvd Ste 301, Oakland, CA 94601  |    EIN: 94-3281780   |info@newschools.org|Accessibility Statement

name: THRIVE Community School
description: THRIVE Community School is a public school that launched in Santa Fe, New Mexico in Fall 2022. The school intends to serve 633 students at full enrollment across grades K-8. THRIVE Community School is a diverse and affirming learning community that embraces social and emotional learning practices and utilizes evidence-based teaching to advance educational equity. We use innovative approaches to drive academic achievement, eliminate disparities in student outcomes, and reinforce a positive self-concept so that all students are positioned to follow paths of their choice.

name: Thrive Industries Incorporated
description: THRIVE! Budgeting for Equity software identifies systemic racism in school spending. Their software compiles solutions that have been empirically proven effective in closing the achievement gap, improving attendance, and reducing disciplinary actions by evaluation science and leaders of color. Then Thrive compares their index of best practices to what districts are actually spending money on. The organization also assess the extent to which students and parents are empowered in budgetary decision-making.

name: Thriving Students Collective
description: Thriving Students Collective® provides engaging digital resources and professional development for individuals and K-12 schools to: 1) Reverse educator and mental health provider burnout, 2) Support families of children with mental health and learning needs, and 3) Provide “learn today, use tomorrow” curriculum for supporting diverse learners.  Our video streaming service,Thrive Hive TV™, brings child development experts into the home and classroom, with short, research-based, practical advice for teaching executive functioning, social-emotional learning, and strategies to support students with disabilities.

name: Timely Schools
description: Timely Schools helps middle and high schools create their master schedules using a modern web-based application that features AI optimization technology. Supported by a team of experienced educators, Timely Schools streamlines the scheduling process to better meet the needs of educators and students.

name: Transformative Learning Solutions
description: Transformative Learning Solutions’ mission is to support the growth and development of teachers and school leaders to create the conditions that improve instruction for historically marginalized students so that they may become independent learners with agency and greater capacity for deeper learning. We do so using the science of learning, culturally responsive instructional practices, and collaborative inquiry. We believe just as school inequity was designed as a system of oppression, we can disrupt it by deliberating designing instruction for equity that builds the brain power of each child regardless of zip code, home language, or income level.

name: trubel&co
description: trubel&co (pronounced “trouble and co”) is a tech-justice nonprofit championing underserved youth to tackle complex societal challenges using equitable data analytics, responsible technology, and inclusive design. Our mission is to accelerate proximate innovators committed to liberation, as we empower the next generation with the opportunity and tools to fix the problems they care about the most. Our flagship program, Mapping Justice, teaches high school youth to design geospatial tools for social change, where students control their own narrative, explore the intersection of Race, Power, & Technology, and create digital apps & dashboards that tackle community-based challenges.

name: True Measure Collaborative
description: The True Measure Collaborative’s mission is to foster collaboration and build educator capacity in service of equitable educational opportunities for all students – especially students with disabilities and multilingual learners. Through the expansion of inclusive education, anti-discriminatory and anti-racist educational programming, and tailored professional development, the TMC catalyzes institutional change that enables educators to remove barriers to excellent educational experiences for all students – especially students who are least served by our current systems.

name: UnboundED
description: UnboundEd equips educators to disrupt predictable achievement patterns and meet the needs of every student. The organization is launching a new technology division to deliver K–12 digital services in reading and math, beginning with the Online Math Academy.

name: University of Colorado, Boulder
description: We are an R&D team within the Institute of Cognitive Science at CU Boulder developing AI tools for education. Through this initiative, we are building a GenAI partner to facilitate collaborative learning in small-group math tutoring.

name: University of Utah
description: The University of Utah, founded in 1850, is Utah’s flagship R1 public research university and an AAU member serving approximately 36,000 students. It advances responsible AI through campus-wide initiatives such as the One U Responsible AI Initiative and integrates AI across disciplines, including education.

name: Unruly Studios
description: Unruly Math turns math practice into movement-based games that get students collaborating and learning through play. It builds confidence, fluency, and engagement — reducing screen time while helping students internalize concepts through active, joyful learning.

name: Uplift K12 LLC
description: Uplift K12 gets to the root of math anxiety and boosts students’ confidence through the use of virtual manipulatives, interactive collaboration, and multiplayer games. We are an enthusiastic group of people with a passion for education and a commitment to improved learning outcomes.

name: Uppercase
description: Uppercase leverages AI to provide curriculum-aligned, personalized teaching advice, resources, and coaching — on demand, when educators need it most.

name: Uprooted Academy
description: Uprooted Academy is enhancing our platform with tools for students with learning differences, training for school counselors, and guidance for families. This includes tailored planning milestones and a directory of university accessibility services to support equitable postsecondary planning.

name: Urban Assembly, Inc.
description: The Urban Assembly (UA) advances students’ economic and social mobility by improving public education. Since 1997, UA has founded more than 20 small, career-themed high schools in New York City, pioneering career-connected education in partnership with NYC Public Schools. UA’s school design and support have led to high-impact learning solutions for schools and districts nationwide.

name: Vertex Partnership Academies
description: Vertex Partnership Academies seeks to develop virtuous high school graduates who have acquired the habits, knowledge and sense of personal agency necessary to lead self-determined, purposeful lives of American and global citizenship. Our vision is to develop internationally-minded, tenacious learners guided by the four cardinal virtues of Courage, Justice, Temperance, and Wisdom.

name: Village of Wisdom
description: Established in 2014, Village of Wisdom’s mission is to liberate and protect the intellectual curiosity and positive racial identity of Black children (Black Genius®). We do this by amplifying the cultural strengths of Black families and creating the conditions for Black parents to co-design culturally affirming and liberatory environments in and outside the classroom. Towards our mission, we are launching the Black Parent Dreamship — a leadership fellowship where parents channel their wisdom into designing and validating culturally affirming and liberatory learning environments for Black learners across the United States.

name: Vimenti
description: Vimenti leads the social sector with access to high-quality and integrated education, health, and economic mobility services for children and families. At Vimenti, we achieve two-generational outcomes by centering families in evidence-based programs and advising policy change efforts to reduce poverty in Puerto Rico.

name: Vimenti
description: Vimenti leads the social sector with access to high-quality and integrated education, health, and economic mobility services for children and families. At Vimenti, they achieve two-generational outcomes by centering families in evidence-based programs and advising policy change efforts to reduce poverty in Puerto Rico. Vimenti will open up their second site in Fall 2023 and they intend to serve 300 students at full enrollment across grades 6-12.

name: Vinn Labs LLC
description: Simili is a voice-powered math platform for grades 3–5 that helps students build positive math identities through real-world problem solving, sense-making, and verbal reasoning. It supports classrooms using Illustrative Mathematics and Building Thinking Classrooms.

name: Virtual Innovators Academy VIANYC (NYC DOE)
description: Virtual Innovators Academy is a New York City and New York State’s first virtual school. We are an interdisciplinary, project-based, competency assessed high school that uses Design Thinking to create technology-based solutions to real-world problems.

name: Voces Unidas de la Montañas
description: Voces Unidas de la Montañas is launching a new parent advocacy initiative to transform rural public education in Colorado by supporting Latino parent leadership at the school and district levels. This initiative will organize and train Latino parents to advocate for policies aimed at increasing student achievement in two school districts: The Roaring Fork and Eagle County School Districts. Voces Unidas will develop Latino parents’ capacities to organize and influence rural school district policy-making by training, preparing, and supporting them to take on leadership roles on school boards and advisory committees. By partnering with parents, superintendents, principals, teachers, and other community organizations, Voces Unidas hopes to create a more equitable environment in rural Colorado where all students thrive.

name: Voice4Equity
description: Voice4Equity was established to increase the number of women and leaders of color at the policy table. Our mission is to disrupt years of systemic bias and inequities by working with women and organizations to expand the diversity of voice through strategic leadership, equity policies and community engagement.

name: Warwick Community School (Town of Warwick)
description: The Town of Warwick is launching a public school that will launch in Warwick, Massachusetts in Fall 2023. The school intends to serve 80 students at full enrollment across grades PK-8.The town is reestablishing local elementary education which the town began in the 1700s. They will utilize their relatively new school building our regional left, provide place based, individualized elementary education for our kids, maintain a tuition relationship with our former district for secondary education, establish relationships with other small and tiny schools and districts, share services with the Town, and eventual establishment of a scattered site / non contiguous central office service.

name: We Love Philly
description: We Love Philly is an out-of-school time program in Philadelphia that aims to empower students with positive community experiences and essential professional skills through mindfulness, volunteerism, entrepreneurship, and content creation. We Love Philly teaches students how to navigate complex emotional stressors, equips them with essential life skills, arranges volunteer experiences to promote community, connects students with local entrepreneurs and business owners to gain expertise, and lastly helps students to create their own brand.

name: Welfie
description: Welfie is a health equity platform that puts community health workers in schools to deliver health education and care coordination. We provide districts with a community schools platform that addresses social determinants of health and education. And families with a consumer health app to navigate health, wellness and access to services.

name: West Dallas STEM School
description: West Dallas STEM School is a public school that launched in Dallas, Texas in Fall 2022. This school intends to serve over 900 students at full enrollment across grades PreK3 through 8th grade. West Dallas STEM School is a 4-way partnership between SMU, Toyota, Dallas ISD, and West Dallas Community partners including families and nonprofit organizations. Using innovative strategies and placed based learning, we support student academic achievement such that students graduate college and career ready for 21st century jobs and beyond.

name: Western Youth Leadership, Engagement, and Empowerment School
description: The Western Youth Leadership, Engagement, and Empowerment Middle School (WYLEES) is a proposed public charter school in Nevada, set to open in fall 2025. Serving downtown Las Vegas, WYLEES will offer project-based learning experiences co-created with students and families, focusing on community improvement through real-world service learning. The school aims to empower future leaders by fostering creativity, collaboration, and civic engagement.

name: xS.T.R.E.A.M. Minds Academy
description: At xS.T.R.E.A.M. Minds Academy in Birmingham, Alabama, our mission is to reimagine learning for all students through S.T.R.E.A.M. exploration, personalized instruction, and social-emotional growth.

name: Young Data Scientists League
description: The Young Data Scientists League (YDSL) empowers every student — especially those historically underserved — to learn and apply data science and AI ethics skills that make a positive difference in their lives and communities.

name: Young Leaders Strong City
description: Young Leaders, Strong City is student-centered organizing that focuses on dismantling institutional and structural racism, providing a historical analysis of inequitable policies and practices, addressing intersectionality in issues and solutions, and envisioning a more equitable society

name: Youth Justice Alliance
description: The Youth Justice Alliance is dedicated to expanding access to legal knowledge, legal support, and legal careers for historically excluded communities. This mission is carried out through YJA’s support for legal education in secondary schools and their fellowship for aspiring first-gen lawyers. The fellowship program supports aspiring lawyers from high school graduation all the way to and through law school matriculation.

name: Abl
description: Abl offers a cloud-based platform that provides district leaders with insights by analyzing the fundamental blueprint for every school - the schedule - and tools to support school leaders to take action on those insights for systemic improvement.

name: Accelerate Learing
description: STEMscopes' mission is to empower teachers to focus on effective instruction, rather than on devoting time to a search for quality resources and materials.

name: Amira Learning

name: BridgeBio
description: BridgeBio develops breakthrough medicines for genetic diseases. Its portfolio of more than 15 drugs, all in various stages of development, addresses everything from genetic skin, cancer- and heart-related issues to renal disease.

name: Bruce
description: Bruce is the trust infrastructure platform for professional services. As an AI-native reputation and discovery layer, it converts real-world relationships and verified performance into structured trust data that powers intelligent search and matching. By scaling trust beyond individual networks, it establishes the system of record for professional trust, aligning market success with real credibility.

name: Bunkerhill Health
description: Bunkerhill Health brings AI algorithms—built by researchers around the world—to widespread adoption in healthcare, helping hospitals find at-risk patients and close critical gaps in care. Its platform scans medical images and EHRs, flags patients for follow-up, and automates care coordination. Bunkerhill improves outcomes, streamlines workflows, and boosts revenue—without adding work for clinicians.

name: Cadence Design Systems
description: Cadence is a leader in electronic systems design, building upon more than 30 years of computational software expertise. The company applies its underlying Intelligent System Design strategy to deliver software, hardware, and IP that turn design concepts into reality.

name: CafePress
description: CafePress works with designers to bring the world millions of designs on hundreds of different products.

name: Caldera
description: Caldera specializes in building high-performance, customizable, and application-specific layer-two blockchains. These custom-built blockchains (Caldera Chains) offer high throughput, low latency, and customizable features for optimizing the performance and user experience of decentralized applications, with the ability to process hundreds of transactions per second and sub-second confirmation times.

name: Captions
description: Captions is an AI-powered creative studio. Their goal is to enable anyone, anywhere to produce studio-quality videos with just a few quick taps.

name: CaptivateIQ
description: CaptivateIQ helps enterprise companies make their sales commissions transparent. With CaptivateIQ, businesses can design and measure incentive plans so their teams feel more connected and motivated to work together.

name: Carbon
description: Carbon is transforming 3D printing through the process of light extrusion. This technology moves beyond basic prototyping to 3D to deliver materials faster than ever before.

name: Catch
description: Catch helps merchants and their customers save by cutting out the credit card companies. By paying directly by bank, customers save merchants on payment processing fees. Merchants pass those savings back to the customer as store credit and drive retention. Everyone wins—except the card companies.

name: Cellworks
description: Cellworks is a personalized medicine platform that uses AI to predict successful matches between patients and drug therapies, which are then clinically validated. This technology drives down costs and provides payors with transparency.

name: Census
description: Census takes models and insights from data warehouses to help go-to-market teams streamline their workflows.

name: Chainguard
description: Chainguard is the safe source for open source. The company’s mission is to make the software supply chain secure by default. Chainguard develops enterprise solutions to mitigate attacks along every stage of the software development cycle without sacrificing developer productivity.

name: Chkk
description: Chkk is a Kubernetes Availability Platform that identifies and prioritizes availability risks before they cause incidents, tracks artifacts and assets across the fleet, and creates pre-verified upgrade plans to remediate risks and speed up upgrades. Platform teams use Chkk to execute safe upgrades, remediate availability risks, monitor unsupported versions, and get visibility into what artifacts are running where. With Chkk, teams learn from others, don’t repeat mistakes, save time and effort, and get more done with fewer resources.

name: Cisco
description: Cisco was one of the first companies to sell commercially successful routers for multiple network protocols. Today Cisco connects people, data and processes through its cloud-computing applications and services. Its hardware, software and services create the internet solutions that make networks possible.

name: Citadel Securities
description: Citadel Securities is a leading global market maker that provides both institutional and retail investors with the liquidity they need to execute transactions across a broad array of equity and fixed income products. The firm has been at the forefront of the modernization of markets and market structures, which has delivered enormous benefits to investors globally. The talent of its extraordinary market making team, combined with its powerful risk management techniques, advanced predictive analytics and superior software engineering have transformed the client experience in the many markets it serves.

name: Citizen
description: Citizen is a personal safety app and mobile protection service.

name: Claim
description: Claim is building a new way for friends to connect and for brands to reward their customers. On Claim, friends earn cash back, exchange rewards, and redeem together by shopping at their favorite brands. The result is a social network that focuses on real-world value, authenticity, and communal experience — rather than manufactured content and reposts. Consumers discover brands on Claim via rewards from friends. For high-growth brands, this word-of-mouth matching creates the perfect introduction to a new customer — a reward from a friend is always better than an ad from an influencer.

name: Clari
description: Clari’s revenue operations platform uses AI to automatically harvest data from multiple enterprise systems across the entire revenue process, with the goal of creating transparency. Their end-to-end sales analytics and forecasting systems connect teams and make revenue more predictable.

name: Clay
description: Clay makes creative tools that help businesses grow by improving their customer research to scale personalized outreach. Its AI agent helps RevOps and growth teams implement any outreach idea.

name: Clever
description: Clever simplifies educational technology for teachers and students. Its platform puts every application in one place, charging app developers, not schools, for the service.

name: Clipboard Health
description: Clipboard Health is an app-based talent marketplace that matches healthcare professionals with open shifts at healthcare facilities near them. Its mission is to lift as many people up the socioeconomic ladder as possible. Short-staffed healthcare facilities represent a long-term problem that became more acute with COVID-19, leading to increased burnout among nurses, less care and access to healthcare for patients, and skyrocketing costs for healthcare facilities.

name: Cobot
description: Cobot is redefining the future of human-robot interaction by building practical cobots that have human-like capabilities.

name: Cockroach Labs
description: Cockroach Labs provides a cloud-native, distributed SQL database for critical enterprise applications.

name: Cohesity
description: Cohesity’s cloud-based platform makes it easy to back up, manage and extract value from enterprise data.

name: Commons
description: Commons is an app and community that offers practical guidance for sustainable living. With the app, anyone can track, reduce, and offset the emissions of everything they buy. Join a community of people shifting systems through our collective spending and choices.

name: Commure
description: Commure (FKA Athelas) is an AI-powered operating system for healthcare providers that connects data and workflows while boosting clinical and financial performance.

name: Confluent
description: Founded by the Apache Kafka team at LinkedIn, Confluent is a streaming data platform that helps companies access enterprise data in real time.

name: Cortex
description: Cortex helps engineering teams improve their service-oriented architecture. It catalogs their services and reveals their performance in terms of quality initiatives, security audits and other tasks.

name: Cove
description: Cove is an AI-assisted workspace that helps you get more done.

name: Cresta
description: Cresta helps businesses save money, increase revenue and build customer loyalty with the only unified platform for human and AI agents built for enterprise scale.

name: Cribl
description: Founded by three ex-Splunkers on a mission to get the most out of machine data, Cribl helps customers easily sift through data to find the nuggets they need. Its innovative and customizable controls route security and machine data where it has the most value—and with a singular focus on assuring customers have complete control to shape their data. As data needs evolve, LogStream evolves too, helping to get the right data, to the right destinations, in the right formats, at the right time.

name: Crosby
description: Crosby is the hybrid AI law company that helps rapidly growing businesses execute faster. Crosby combines the speed and intelligence of AI with the safety of lawyers-in-the-loop to review contracts in minutes. Crosby negotiates MSAs, DPAs and NDAs for fast-growing businesses, using speed and intelligence to reach agreement faster, and unblock sales teams.

name: CUE Labs
description: CUE Labs, the company behind the CUE open-source project, builds a Configuration Control Plane that turns configuration from a source of risk into a source of reliability. It enables teams and organizations to understand, audit and validate all data, from build-time configurations to runtime payloads, to prevent outages and ensure compliance.

name: Curie
description: Curie is a voice-driven AI assistant for clinicians.

name: Cyera
description: Cyera gives organizations instant Data Reality, taking the guesswork out of cloud data security. It enables security teams to have a complete, current, and correct picture of their data reality across all clouds and datastores. Running out-of-band and without agents, Cyera instantly provides companies a strong baseline for all security, risk management, and compliance efforts and ensures the entire organization operates with the same policies and guardrails.

name: Dagster
description: Dagster is a data orchestration platform for the development, production and observation of data assets.

name: Dashlane
description: Dashlane secures users’ digital identity across a wide range of platforms and devices. The Dashlane app automatically fills and stores passwords, personal data and payment details, and includes a suite of tools to help consumers protect their identity online.

name: Day.ai
description: Day.ai is an AI-native CRM that brings the voice of the customer into every decision. Its product combines a meeting assistant, CRM and knowledge base into a single unified solution.

name: dbt Labs
description: dbt uses integrated analytics to help data teams work like software engineers to ship data faster. Users can work directly within their data warehouse to quickly produce trusted datasets for reporting, ML modeling and operational workflows.

name: Decart
description: The Decart efficient AI platform provides an order of magnitude improvement in both training and inferencing of the largest generative models. Leveraging these advanced capabilities, Decart is able to train foundational generative interactive models and make it accessible for everyone in real-time.

name: Delphi
description: Delphi lets experts scale their time and knowledge through interactive digital minds. Its AI-powered avatars mirror users’ speaking and thinking patterns from their existing content, enabling anyone to provide valuable advice globally through voice, text and video.

name: Deno
description: Deno is building application development for the modern web.

name: Despegar
description: Despegar is the leading travel technology company in Latin America.

name: Dolls Kill
description: Dolls Kill is a global fashion brand championing radically unapologetic self-expression. By curating the most unique assortment of brands across a range of looks, Dolls Kill is building a platform to empower the next generation.

name: Domino Data Lab
description: Domino Data Lab’s platforms help data scientists build and deploy AI applications. Its tool not only trains models, but monitors performance once they’ve gone live.

name: DoorDash
description: DoorDash is a technology and logistics company that started with on-demand door-to-door delivery. It empowers local businesses to connect with people through their platform of diners and dashers.

name: Doppler
description: Doppler is a central source of truth for env vars and secrets. It enables sharing of API keys, database urls and certs across users’ team and infra.

name: Draftea
description: Draftea is the premier real-money gaming platform in the Spanish-speaking world. Sports fans can easily place bets on their favorite teams, players and matches while enjoying an engaging live experience that brings the action closer than ever.

name: Drift
description: Drift is the world’s leading conversational marketing platform. It combines chat, email, video and automation to remove friction from the buying experience. Customers use Drift to upgrade the buyer experience, generate more qualified leads, and dramatically accelerate the sales cycle.

name: Dropbox
description: Dropbox is a collaboration and storage platform transforming the way people and teams work together.

name: Dust
description: Dust is an AI operating system for smart teams. It reshapes information management in the workplace by combining multiple large language models to process information, making it readily available when needed and in the format that makes sense. Dust can augment humans, enhance productivity, foster diversity and facilitate a more dynamic, personalized work experience.

name: Edra
description: Edra creates, deploys and updates executable knowledge for AI agents in the enterprise. It connects to existing source systems like ServiceNow, Zendesk and Outlook, reverse-engineers how a business actually operates and codifies that knowledge into clear, auditable instructions. AI agents then automate reliably, following the company’s actual processes. As teams keep working, Edra continuously learns, keeping its knowledge library current.

name: EDX Markets
description: EDX Markets is a new digital asset exchange designed to meet the needs of both crypto-native firms and the world’s largest financial institutions. EDX will enable safer, faster and more efficient trading of digital assets, leveraging best practices from traditional financial markets on a purpose-built crypto platform.

name: Electronic Arts
description: Electronic Arts was a pioneer of the early home computer games industry. Now a global leader in interactive entertainment software, EA delivers games, content and online services for internet-connected consoles, personal computers, mobile phones and tablets. EA is best known for franchises like The Sims and Madden NFL.

name: Elevate
description: Elevate is a new type of cognitive-training tool designed to build communication and analytical skills. Users receive a personalized gamified program that evolves with their performance, encouraging them to keep learning.

name: ElevenLabs
description: ElevenLabs is a Voice AI research and deployment company making content universally accessible in any language and voice. They use natural speech synthesis to enable users to create and design AI voices across a vast swathe of languages, accents, emotions, and delivery styles. The company’s in-house research drives the development of its technology, which includes tools for audiobook publishing, voiceovers, AI dubbing, and applications in entertainment, education, accessibility, and personal use.

name: Elyn
description: Elyn provides a return management and exchange portal that helps brands optimize their online profitability.

name: Enter
description: Enter helps large companies use AI to boost efficiency of legal teams with exhaustive document analysis that leads to winning more cases.

name: Eon
description: Eon transforms your cloud backups into an accessible, useful asset. Use Eon to back up your entire cloud environment with automatic mapping and classification, so you only store what you need. Nothing more, nothing less.

name: Espresso Systems
description: Espresso Systems provides fast, reliable transaction confirmations for L2 crypto chains. Its network supports open, permissionless and composable services with decentralized sequencing and low-cost data availability.

name: Ethos
description: Ethos makes it easier, faster and better for everyone to get life insurance. Ethos leverages deep technology and data science to make the process more convenient. It has issued billions in coverage each month while eliminating traditional barriers.

name: Eventbrite
description: Eventbrite is a global self-service ticketing platform for live experiences. From music festivals and marathons to conferences and fundraisers, anyone can use the platform to discover enriching events.

name: Evernote
description: Evernote brings your life’s work together in one digital workspace. It makes it easy to capture and organize notes and collaborate in a single workspace.

name: Evervault
description: Evervault is building the internet infrastructure for data privacy. Its simple, secure cloud infrastructure can be set up in minutes.

name: Evy
description: Evy provides a turnkey product protection solution that can be fully integrated into a merchant’s existing sales channels. Customers benefit from a first-class service that gives them peace of mind from the moment of purchase. Evy is committed to facilitating product repairs over their replacement, by developing additional services such as buyback, recycling or reconditioning.

name: Factory
description: Factory is bringing autonomy to software engineering. Factory’s Droids robustly, reliably, and autonomously solve engineering tasks, like code review, documentation, testing, debugging, coding, and project management.

name: Faire
description: Faire uses data and machine learning to connect entrepreneurs worldwide. Its online wholesale marketplaces level the playing field so small businesses can compete with the retail giants.

name: fal
description: fal is redefining the generative-media infrastructure layer for the AI era. As the leading platform for real-time video, image, audio and 3D generation, fal is building the world’s first Generative Media Cloud—designed to give developers and enterprises access to more than 600 production-ready models through a unified API. fal’s systems already support hundreds of millions of end users globally, a clear demonstration of the platform’s ability to operate at extraordinary scale.

name: Faraway
description: Faraway is an interoperable gaming ecosystem for players, creators and studios. It makes it easy for creators to integrate the web3 open economy into their games and monetize through the Faraway community.

name: FastAPI Labs
description: FastAPI Labs helps developers build apps and APIs with open source and cloud-based tools.

name: Fides
description: Fides is the first ever Management Governance Platform (MGP) enabling companies to globally monitor, execute and automate their corporate governance duties. On one hand, Fides acts as the single-source-of-truth for all things entity and makes it easy to find key info per entity in a structured, actionable and visualised way. On top, Fides streamlines the execution of workflows for governance-related decisions, particularly through meetings, resolutions and approvals among the c-level suite. Fides customers benefit from reducing director’s liabilities, lower spend on legal resources, accelerated deal-readiness in DD and M&A processes as well as preventing governance scandals.

name: Figma
description: Figma is the first web-based collaborative design tool. Multiple people can jump in a file at the same time, so they can design, prototype, copy code and gather feedback in one place. Figma has simplified the design process for teams of designers, developers, product managers, marketers and others at thousands of companies including Uber, Square, Twitter and GitHub.

name: Filecoin
description: Filecoin is a decentralized storage network designed to store humanity’s most important information. The network verifies that data is stored securely through cryptographic proofs.

name: Fin
description: Fin is a next-generation payments platform built for high-value, global, and instant transactions. Powered by stablecoins, Fin enables users and businesses to move millions of dollars instantly—whether to other Fin users, directly into bank accounts, or across crypto rails. By combining the speed of crypto with the reliability of traditional finance, Fin reimagines how money moves worldwide. If banks and payment products could be rebuilt from the ground up today, they would look like Fin.

name: Finch
description: Finch is a modern pre-litigation platform built for personal injury law firms, combining elite paralegals and advanced AI agents to handle administrative work. By solving for intake, case management, and demand drafting, Finch enables plaintiff firms to serve more clients while providing a high quality client experience and reducing the access to justice gap in the U.S.

name: Fintual
description: Fintual provides easy access to a balanced investment portfolio through its easy-to-use app. Launched in Chile in 2018, and Mexico in 2021, Fintual has a regulatory license that allows it to offer mostly global ETFs.

name: Fireblocks
description: Fireblocks is an enterprise-grade platform with a secure infrastructure for moving, storing and issuing digital assets. It lets exchanges, custodians, banks, trading desks and hedge funds securely scale digital asset operations through the Fireblocks Network and MPC-based Wallet Infrastructure. Fireblocks’ insurance policy covers assets in storage and transit.

name: FireEye
description: FireEye brought a fresh perspective to network security when it launched in 2004. Today FireEye’s virtual machine-based security platform protects countless Fortune 500 companies and governments worldwide against cyberattacks and malware.

name: Firetiger
description: Firetiger is the operations layer for the agentic coding era. Its AI agents combine production observability data, codebase understanding and business context to identify reliability and performance issues, then steer coding agents to fix them autonomously.

name: Fireworks AI
description: Fireworks AI is a high-performance inference platform for developers and enterprises to run open-source LLMs and image models or to fine-tune and deploy their own.

name: Flagship
description: Flagship enables creators to set up their own curated boutiques, without having to deal with inventory, customer service or sales tax. They partner with brands to power a large catalog that creators can curate from for their audience and earn a commission on every sale. When consumers discover and buy products from creator-owned stores, the platform automatically propagates the orders to partner brands and settles the payment between brands and creators. Flagship is setting a standard for a privacy-forward customer acquisition that is centered humans and entrepreneurs.

name: Flapping Airplanes
description: Flapping Airplanes is a foundational AI research lab devoted to solving the data efficiency problem.

name: Flextronics
description: Flextronics is the manufacturing partner of choice that helps a diverse customer base design and build products that improve the world.

name: Flow Engineering
description: Re-usable rockets, humanoid robotics, self-driving cars: Flow is a collaborative hardware development platform that enables teams to design, build, test and iterate massively complex hardware products at software speeds. Companies like Rivian, Joby, Astrianis and Radiant Nuclear are built on Flow.

name: Formation Bio
description: Formation Bio is a tech and AI-native pharma company differentiated by radically more efficient drug development.

name: Found
description: Found gives self-employed people the support and confidence to work for themselves. It offers an all-in-one business banking, bookkeeping and tax solution for self-employed businesses.

name: Freed
description: Freed is an AI clinician assistant that automates visit preparation, documentation, EHR interactions and other burdensome clinical tasks. Freed is tackling the crisis of clinician burnout by giving clinicians valuable time back in their day.

name: Front
description: Front is the AI-powered customer service platform built for collaboration. It brings all support channels into a modern, intuitive workspace where teams can collaborate on requests, automate manual processes and delight customers.

name: FTX
description: FTX.COM is a cryptocurrency exchange. It offers derivatives, options and volatility products, tokenized stocks, prediction markets, leveraged tokens and an OTC desk. Update: November 9, 2022 A liquidity crunch has created solvency risk for FTX and its future is uncertain. Many have been affected by this unexpected turn of events. For Sequoia, our fiduciary responsibility is to our LPs. To that end, we shared this letter with them today regarding our investment in FTX. For FTX, we believe its fiduciary responsibility is first to its customers, and second to its shareholders. As such, FTX is exploring all opportunities to ensure its customers are able to recover their funds as quickly as possible. Editor’s Note On September 22, 2022, we published a profile story on FTX founder Sam Bankman-Fried. The week of November 7th, we learned of the company’s liquidity crunch. On November 9th we sent a letter to our LPs with our updated outlook on FTX, and we removed our profile story on SBF the next morning. On November 11th, Bankman-Fried stepped down as CEO and FTX filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy.

name: FutureAdvisor
description: FutureAdvisor was a financial services provider for individuals, acquired by BlackRock.

name: Gadget
description: Gadget is a serverless stack for ecommerce app developers. It connects to any ecommerce platform instantly, and enables developers to build apps in a fraction of the time by skipping the busywork.

name: Garner Health
description: Garner Health helps employers enrich employee benefits and reduce costs without changing plans or networks. Its proprietary claims database identifies the doctors with the most accurate diagnoses and the best patient outcomes and makes it easy to find convenient, in-network providers.

name: Gather
description: Gather is building a social platform that fosters authentic connections. Its platform combines video chat with virtual spaces to make conversations more human.

name: Getir
description: Getir revolutionizes last-mile delivery with its grocery delivery proposition. Getir delivers a selection of 1,500 everyday items wherever its customers wish within minutes, seven days a week, day and night. Getir, which launched in London in January 2021, also owns sub-brands GetirFood, GetirMore and GetirWater.

name: Gigi
description: Gigi is the AI social network redefining how people connect, signal status and unlock opportunity. Gigi makes context king—who you know, who trusts you, what you’ve built and how others perceive you—to reveal your social capital.

name: GitHub
